Section 41 covers illegal business and insolvency of all but one partner.

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: How does a passive (sleeping) partner contribute?
mcqData.explanation:
Sleeping partners provide funds but do not participate in daily work.

Correct Answer: Investing Capital

mcqData.text: Stem: Priyom & Palash (1L, 70k). Joy joins with 50k on condition of NO management. Q: Joy is what type?
mcqData.explanation:
Since Joy provides capital but doesn't manage, he is a Passive/Sleeping partner.

Correct Answer: Passive

mcqData.text: Stem: Total Capital 2.2L (1L+70k+50k). Profit 66k. Q: Applicable- (i. Optional, ii. Priyom gets 30k, iii. Joy's liability limited)
mcqData.explanation:
Calculation: (66,000 / 2.2L) * 1L = 30,000. Joy's liability is unlimited unless registered as limited.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Who allows reputation use in exchange for benefits?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Nominal partners provide goodwill/name for a share or fee.

Correct Answer: Nominal

mcqData.text: A minor must declare his intent within how many days of becoming an adult?
mcqData.explanation:
A minor has 6 months (180 days) to publicly declare if they will stay in the firm.

Correct Answer: 180

mcqData.text: Minimum number of "General Partners" in a limited partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
To keep the firm legal, at least one partner must have unlimited liability.

Correct Answer: 1

mcqData.text: How does a court-ordered dissolution happen?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 44(a) covers insanity (Mostishko Bikriti) as a ground for court dissolution.

Correct Answer: Partner goes insane

mcqData.text: Stem: Rod & friends form a firm to build a foot-over bridge (9 months). Q: Dissolution method?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 42(b) says a firm dissolves upon the completion of the specific task.

Correct Answer: Special event

mcqData.text: Who leaves their capital as a loan after retirement?
mcqData.explanation:
A Quasi-partner (Apatdrishti) is a retired partner who leaves their funds in the business.

Correct Answer: Quasi-partner

mcqData.text: Stem: Three friends XYZ formed a farm for pesticide-free vegetables. To avoid legal issues, they now want a new entity with separate status. Q: What type is XYZ farm based on ownership?
mcqData.explanation:
A business formed by friends to share profit/loss without separate legal status is a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Why can a partnership business last for a long time?
mcqData.explanation:
Mutual trust (Parasparik Astha) is the foundation that keeps partners together.

Correct Answer: Mutual trust & confidence

mcqData.text: Stem: 5 friends started a business. They took Mr. Susan as a partner to use his reputation. Q: What type of partner is Mr. Susan?
mcqData.explanation:
Nominal (Nammatro) partners allow the firm to use their name/reputation without investing much capital.

Correct Answer: Nominal

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) The firm needs a loan but faces issues with the bank. Q: How can they solve the problem? (i. Loans from relatives, ii. Personal bank loans, iii. Selling shares)
mcqData.explanation:
Partnerships cannot sell shares to the public; only companies can do that.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: What is the minimum number of members in a banking partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
For any partnership (general or banking), the minimum number of members is 2.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: Stem: Sunny, Johny, Rony invested 1.5L, 1L, and 50k. Sunny will not bear more liability than his capital. Q: Sunny is what type?
mcqData.explanation:
A partner whose liability is restricted to his investment is a Limited (Seemaboddho) partner.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Loss is 6L. Q: In this firm- (i. Limited partner exists, ii. Rony's liability 1.5L, iii. Johny's liability 2L)
mcqData.explanation:
Since Sunny is limited, the others (General partners) have unlimited liability to cover the loss.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Partnership business in Bangladesh is formed under which year's law?
mcqData.explanation:
The governing law is the Partnership Act of 1932.

Correct Answer: 1932

mcqData.text: Stem: Rony, Johny, Mony are partners. Johny's liability is not more than his invested capital. Q: What type of partnership is this?
mcqData.explanation:
A partnership containing at least one limited partner is a Limited Partnership.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Stem: Bacchu, Saju, Sani have an oral agreement. Sani invests capital but doesn't manage. Q: Sani is what type?
mcqData.explanation:
Investing capital but not participating in management defines a Sleeping (Nidrito) partner.

Correct Answer: Sleeping

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) They cannot take legal action against a debtor. Q: To solve this, they should- (i. Written agreement, ii. Register deed, iii. Appoint arbitrator)
mcqData.explanation:
An unregistered firm cannot sue a third party for more than 100 Taka.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: What is the relationship between partners in a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership is a "Contractual Relationship" (Chuktiboddho Shomporko).

Correct Answer: Contractual

mcqData.text: What type of partner is a minor in a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
A minor's liability is always limited to his share in the firm.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Minimum members in a banking partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Minimum for any partnership is 2.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: Bashir resigned but kept his money as a loan. He still visits. Bashir is what type?
mcqData.explanation:
A retired partner who leaves his capital as a loan is a Quasi-partner (Apatdrishti).

Correct Answer: Quasi-partner

mcqData.text: Which type of partner will receive profit from the business?
mcqData.explanation:
Limited partners get profit; Quasi-partners get interest on their loan.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: What type of partner is a minor?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Minors are admitted only for benefits with limited liability.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Essential elements of partnership are- (i. Multiple members, ii. Contractual relation, iii. Registration)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration is NOT mandatory/essential for the existence of a partnership.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Stem: Rita, Mita, Setu have a registered firm. Suddenly Rita dies. Q: Dissolution method?
mcqData.explanation:
Under Section 42(c), a firm dissolves upon the death of a partner (unless agreed otherwise).

Correct Answer: Special event

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Z retired, left capital as a loan, and gave public notice. Q: Mr. Z is what type?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Retired partner with loan = Quasi-partner.

Correct Answer: Quasi-partner

mcqData.text: Stem: Mr. Z retired, kept capital as loan, and gave public notice. Q: What applies to Mr. Z? (i. He is a creditor, ii. He gets profit, iii. Not liable for post-retirement loans)
mcqData.explanation:
A Quasi-partner (creditor) gets interest, not profit. Public notice clears them from future liabilities.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Minimum number of members in a partnership business?
mcqData.explanation:
By law, at least two persons are needed to form a partnership.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: What is the main basis of a partnership business?
mcqData.explanation:
"Contract" (Chukti) is the fundamental basis, not status or birth.

Correct Answer: Contract

mcqData.text: What type of partner is a minor?
mcqData.explanation:
A minor's liability is limited to their investment in the firm.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Partners must be- (i. Adult, ii. Educated, iii. Of sound mind)
mcqData.explanation:
Education is not a legal requirement, but sanity and being an adult are.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: What is the relationship between partners called?
mcqData.explanation:
The legal relation between persons who have agreed to share profits is "Partnership".

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: Shelly retired but didn't give public notice. Mafiz supplied goods thinking she is still a partner. Q: Shelly's type?
mcqData.explanation:
If someone represents themselves (or allows it) as a partner, they are a partner by "Estoppel" (Protibondh).

Correct Answer: Estoppel

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) For Mafiz's dues, Shelly-
mcqData.explanation:
Partners are jointly and severally liable for the firm's debts while appearing as partners.

Correct Answer: Jointly liable

mcqData.text: Which section of the 1932 Act mentions "Partnership at Will"?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 7 of the Partnership Act defines 'Optional' or 'At Will' partnership.

Correct Answer: 7

mcqData.text: Which business is most risky regarding liability?
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership has "Unlimited Liability," making it highly risky for personal assets.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Advantages of Partnership: (i. Collective effort, ii. Perpetual existence, iii. Risk sharing)
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership does NOT have perpetual existence; it can dissolve easily.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Which partnership can be dissolved by just a written notice?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 43 states 'Partnership at Will' can be dissolved by any partner via notice.

Correct Answer: Optional/At Will

mcqData.text: Stem: Y died. X and Z admit Y's minor son. Q: Y's son is what type?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Minors are always admitted as limited partners.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Objective of keeping the minor? (i. Provide benefits, ii. Keep business continuous, iii. Training)
mcqData.explanation:
Minors are admitted primarily for their benefits, not to maintain "continuity" like a company.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Stem: X, Y, Z had no clear agreement on profit sharing. Q: If written, what is it called?
mcqData.explanation:
A written partnership agreement is formally called a "Partnership Deed" (Chuktipotro).

Correct Answer: Partnership Deed

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) To solve profit disputes and legal inability to sue- (i. Written contract, ii. Registration, iii. Arbitrator)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration is required to sue debtors; Written contract solves profit disputes.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Minimum number of members in a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) 2 is the minimum.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: What is the constitution/charter of a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
The Partnership Deed acts as the governing document or constitution of the firm.

Correct Answer: Partnership Deed

mcqData.text: Partnership agreement can be- (i. Oral, ii. Written, iii. Registered)
mcqData.explanation:
Any of these forms are valid, though written/registered is legally stronger.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Which is the main/core document of a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
The Partnership Deed (Chuktipotro) is the most vital document.

Correct Answer: Partnership Deed

mcqData.text: Which Partnership Act is currently effective in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation:
The base law is the 1932 Partnership Act.

Correct Answer: 1932

mcqData.text: Stem: Chandra, Champa, and Boby start a business. Profit-sharing is not mentioned in the deed. Q: What type of business is this?
mcqData.explanation:
Multiple owners with a deed/agreement defines a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Profit is 75,000. Q: How much will Boby receive?
mcqData.explanation:
If the deed is silent on profit sharing, all partners get an EQUAL share. (75,000 / 3 = 25,000).

Correct Answer: 25,000

mcqData.text: Disadvantages of partnership: (i. Unlimited liability, ii. Lack of group effort, iii. Uncertainty of stability)
mcqData.explanation:
Group effort is an advantage, not a disadvantage.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Which is a characteristic of a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
Trust is the "soul" of partnership; perpetual existence belongs to companies.

Correct Answer: Mutual trust & confidence

mcqData.text: A partner who does not actively participate in management is called?
mcqData.explanation:
Sleeping/Passive partners only provide capital but don't work.

Correct Answer: Sleeping

mcqData.text: Advantages of partnership: (i. Easy formation, ii. Perpetual existence, iii. Risk sharing)
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Partnership lacks perpetual existence.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Which partnership mentions a specific duration in the deed?
mcqData.explanation:
Specific (Nirdishto) partnership is for a fixed period or project.

Correct Answer: Specific

mcqData.text: Under which year's law is a partnership dissolved?
mcqData.explanation:
Both formation and dissolution follow the 1932 Act.

Correct Answer: 1932

mcqData.text: Compulsory dissolution: (i. Illegal business, ii. All but one bankrupt, iii. Death of a member)
mcqData.explanation:
Death is a 'Special Event' dissolution (Sec 42), not 'Compulsory' (Sec 41).

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Which partner does NOT bear unlimited liability?
mcqData.explanation:
A minor (Nabalok) is only liable up to their share in the firm.

Correct Answer: Minor

mcqData.text: Stability of partnership depends on- (i. Will of partners, ii. Event of death, iii. Law enforcement)
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership stability is tied to the partners' lives and consent.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Who plays the main role in formation and management?
mcqData.explanation:
General/Active partners handle the core formation and daily operations.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Which partner does NOT bear unlimited liability?
mcqData.explanation:
Nominal partners usually don't bear liability unless they represent themselves as active. (Note: Most boards treat 'Minor' as the standard answer for this).

Correct Answer: Nominal

mcqData.text: Partners are liable for their work-
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership liability is both Joint and Several (Sec 25).

Correct Answer: Individually & Jointly

mcqData.text: Which section mentions "Partnership at Will"?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 7 defines Partnership at Will (Oichhik).

Correct Answer: Sec 7

mcqData.text: Partnership Deed-
mcqData.explanation:
The law allows oral agreements; written/reg is recommended but not mandatory.

Correct Answer: None is compulsory

mcqData.text: Prevailing Partnership Law in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat for Emphasis) 1932 Act.

Correct Answer: 1932

mcqData.text: Main basis of partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) "Contract" is the root of partnership.

Correct Answer: Contract

mcqData.text: Max members in a banking partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
Banking partnership limit is 10 members.

Correct Answer: 10

mcqData.text: What is a partner called who does not participate in management?
mcqData.explanation:
Passive (Nishkriyo) or Sleeping partners only invest capital but do not work.

Correct Answer: Passive/Sleeping

mcqData.text: Stem: Mizan Traders uses Shohel Chowdhury's name as a partner. Shohel stays silent. Q: What type of partner is Shohel?
mcqData.explanation:
Partner by Estoppel (Protibondh) allows their name to be used or stays silent when referred to as a partner.

Correct Answer: Estoppel

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) For such a loan, Shohel Chowdhury will be liable-
mcqData.explanation:
Under Section 25, partners (including by estoppel) are jointly and severally liable to third parties.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: A partner whose liability is unlimited but plays no key role in management?
mcqData.explanation:
Passive partners have unlimited liability unless the firm is registered as "Limited."

Correct Answer: Passive

mcqData.text: Stem: Pinky, Rinky, Jannat. Pinky plays the main role in management. Q: Pinky is what type of partner?
mcqData.explanation:
Active/General (Sadharon) partners manage the firm and have unlimited liability.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Rinky goes insane (insanity). Q: Method of dissolution?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 44(a) states a court may dissolve a firm if a partner becomes of unsound mind.

Correct Answer: By Court order

mcqData.text: In which document are the internal rules of a company written? (Note: Contextual Outlier)
mcqData.explanation:
Though a company question, it appears in some mixed board sets. Articles of Association (Shonghovidhi) manage internal rules.

Correct Answer: Articles of Assoc.

mcqData.text: What is mentioned in Section 58 of the Partnership Act 1932?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 58 details the procedure for the "Registration" (Nibondhon) of a firm.

Correct Answer: Registration

mcqData.text: Stem: Mrs. Amena, Saleha & sisters. Sokhina is skilled and taken as a partner without capital. Q: Sokhina is what type?
mcqData.explanation:
A "Worker Partner" (Kormi) provides skills/labor instead of capital.

Correct Answer: Worker

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Saleha failed to transfer her share to her sister. Q: Why? (i. Lack of trust, ii. Difficulty in transfer, iii. Unlimited liability)
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership shares cannot be transferred to outsiders without consent of all partners.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Which partnership is dissolved via a written notice?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 43 covers dissolution of "Partnership at Will" (Oichhik) via notice.

Correct Answer: Optional

mcqData.text: Whose consent is needed to change the terms of a partnership deed?
mcqData.explanation:
Any change in the deed requires the unanimous consent of all partners.

Correct Answer: All Partners

mcqData.text: What acts as the guideline/director for managing a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
The "Partnership Deed" (Chuktipotro) is the operational manual of the firm.

Correct Answer: Deed

mcqData.text: A minor can be which type of partner?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Minors have liability limited to their share only.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Which partner manages but does not invest capital?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Worker partners provide labor, not funds.

Correct Answer: Worker

mcqData.text: Essential characteristics: (i. Multiple members, ii. Contractual, iii. Registration)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration is not a mandatory/essential legal requirement for existence.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Compulsory dissolution occurs if- (i. Partner goes insane, ii. All but one bankrupt, iii. Business is illegal)
mcqData.explanation:
Insanity is a court-ordered ground (Sec 44), not compulsory (Sec 41).

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: Partnership is managed by- (i. All, ii. One on behalf of all, iii. Max capital provider)
mcqData.explanation:
This reflects the principle of Mutual Agency (Sec 4).

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Which is the core document of a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat for Emphasis) The Deed is the primary document.

Correct Answer: Deed

mcqData.text: Max members in a general partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
General limit is 20; banking limit is 10.

Correct Answer: 20

mcqData.text: Minimum number of members in a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
Under the 1932 Act, a partnership must have at least 2 members.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: Which of these are considered business organizations? (i. Star Club Ltd, ii. ABC Poultry, iii. Polly Nursing Home)
mcqData.explanation:
Profit-oriented production or service entities are all business organizations.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Partners must be- (i. Educated, ii. Of sound mind, iii. Adult)
mcqData.explanation:
Legally, a partner must be a major (adult) and sane. Education is not a requirement.

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: What type of partner is a minor?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Minors are only admitted for benefits with limited liability.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: For which business is registration NOT mandatory?
mcqData.explanation:
Registration of a partnership is optional in Bangladesh.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Which business has the highest liability risk?
mcqData.explanation:
Partners have "Joint and Several" unlimited liability, making it riskier than a sole trader.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: Rana, Mona, Kabir (10k, 15k, 20k). No profit ratio in deed. Profit: 48,000. Q: Rana's share?
mcqData.explanation:
Silent deed = Equal sharing. 48,000 / 3 = 16,000.

Correct Answer: 16,000

mcqData.text: Prevailing Partnership Law in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation:
(Standard Answer) The 1932 Act.

Correct Answer: 1932

mcqData.text: What is the core basis of a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat for Emphasis) Contract (Chukti) is the basis.

Correct Answer: Contract

mcqData.text: What can cause the dissolution of a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 42(c) states the death of a partner dissolves the firm (unless otherwise agreed).

Correct Answer: Death of partner

mcqData.text: Minimum members in a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat) Minimum is 2.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: Main basis of partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
While trust is the 'soul', the legal basis is always the 'Contract'.

Correct Answer: Contract

mcqData.text: A partnership agreement can be- (i. Oral, ii. Written, iii. Registered)
mcqData.explanation:
All three forms are valid under the 1932 Act.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Partnership is better than Sole trader because- (i. More capital, ii. Collective decision, iii. Owner's freedom)
mcqData.explanation:
Owners lose individual freedom in a partnership as decisions are shared.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Stem: Jamil & Jahid admit deceased partner Joynul's minor son. Q: The son is what type?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Minors = Limited (Seemaboddho).

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Why was the minor admitted? (i. To provide benefits, ii. To keep firm's condition, iii. To consume his assets)
mcqData.explanation:
Taking a minor is for their benefit, not for consuming their assets.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Stem: W, X, Y, Z formed a firm under 1932 Act. Q: What type of ownership is this?
mcqData.explanation:
Mention of the 1932 Act confirms it's a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: W, X (Capital + Mgmt), Y (Experience, No Capital), Z (Name for fee). Q: Partner types? (i. General, ii. Worker, iii. Nominal)
mcqData.explanation:
W, X are General; Y is Worker; Z is Nominal.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Main basis of partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) "Contract" is the root.

Correct Answer: Contract

mcqData.text: What type of partner is a minor?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Minors are Limited partners.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Stem: Firm introduces Mr. X as a partner. He stays silent despite knowing it. Q: What type of partner is Mr. X?
mcqData.explanation:
Partner by Estoppel (Protibondh) occurs when someone knowingly stays silent while being represented as a partner.

Correct Answer: Estoppel

mcqData.text: With which business is the word 'Contract' (Chukti) most associated?
mcqData.explanation:
Contract is the legal foundation of a partnership (Section 5, 1932 Act).

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: Ahsan & 10 friends. Ahsan agrees on the condition of liability limited to 3L. Q: Business type?
mcqData.explanation:
A firm where at least one partner has limited liability is a Limited (Seemaboddho) Partnership.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Under which section does a partnership lose legal benefits if not registered?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 69 details the legal consequences/disabilities of non-registration.

Correct Answer: 69

mcqData.text: Whose consent is needed to change deed conditions?
mcqData.explanation:
Unanimous consent of all partners is required for any deed modification.

Correct Answer: Partners

mcqData.text: Who participates in business without supplying capital?
mcqData.explanation:
Worker (Kormi) partners contribute labor/skill instead of financial capital.

Correct Answer: Worker

mcqData.text: If 2 out of 3 partners are declared bankrupt, the dissolution will be-
mcqData.explanation:
Section 41 states a firm MUST dissolve if all but one partner become insolvent.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: Stem: Abdur Rahim & 3 friends start a garment factory based on mutual understanding. Q: Organization type?
mcqData.explanation:
Multiple people starting a business via understanding/contract is a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Hiring large unemployed workforce. Q: Which environment factor?
mcqData.explanation:
Availability of human resources/labor is a key social environment factor.

Correct Answer: Social

mcqData.text: A court can dissolve a partnership if-
mcqData.explanation:
Section 44(d) allows court dissolution if a partner willfully/persistently breaks the agreement.

Correct Answer: Persistent breach of deed

mcqData.text: Stem: Kayes & Himel (90k, 60k). Later Masud joins (50k). No mention of profit ratio. Q: Organization type?
mcqData.explanation:
Ownership shared among multiple partners is a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Profit is 42,000. Q: How much profit will Masud get?
mcqData.explanation:
If the deed is silent, profit is shared EQUALLY. (42,000 / 3 = 14,000).

Correct Answer: 14,000

mcqData.text: Which partner participates in management?
mcqData.explanation:
General/Active partners take charge of daily operations.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Compulsory dissolution happens when- (i. Insanity, ii. All but one bankrupt, iii. Illegal business)
mcqData.explanation:
Insanity is a ground for Court dissolution (Section 44), not Compulsory (Section 41).

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: What type of partner is a minor?
mcqData.explanation:
(Standard Answer) Minors are Limited (Seemaboddho) partners.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Regarding Limited Partnerships- (i. All limited, ii. At least one limited, iii. At least two unlimited)
mcqData.explanation:
Law requires at least one general (unlimited) partner in a limited firm. (Options are slightly tricky, check Board standard).

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: If profit-sharing ratio is not in the deed, it must be distributed-
mcqData.explanation:
Silent Deed = Equal Distribution Rule.

Correct Answer: Equally

mcqData.text: Which are NOT characteristics of a partnership? (i. Contractual, ii. Certainty of stability, iii. Legal entity)
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership lacks stability and separate legal entity status.

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: Inheritance business (children sharing profit) is not partnership because-
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership must arise from a contract (Section 5), not inheritance.

Correct Answer: No contractual relation

mcqData.text: Stem: Kamal, Bimal, Peter. Bimal dies. They admit his minor son Amal. Q: Amal's type?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Minors are always Limited partners.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Stem: Amal (minor) is admitted after his father Bimal's death. Q: What is the objective of keeping the minor? (i. Providing benefits, ii. Keeping business size, iii. Developing as a businessman)
mcqData.explanation:
Minors are primarily admitted for their benefit and to gain business experience.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Which partnership dissolves via a simple written notice?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 43 states 'Partnership at Will' (Oichhik) can be dissolved by any partner via notice.

Correct Answer: Optional/At Will

mcqData.text: Which partner invests capital AND participates in management?
mcqData.explanation:
General/Active (Sadharon) partners handle both funds and daily operations.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Characteristics of Limited Partnership: (i. Has unlimited partners, ii. Has limited partners, iii. Uses 'Ltd.' in name)
mcqData.explanation:
Unlike companies, limited partnerships do not use "Ltd." at the end of their name.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Prevailing Partnership Law in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation:
The governing law is the Partnership Act of 1932.

Correct Answer: 1932

mcqData.text: Which business has no separate legal entity?
mcqData.explanation:
Legally, a firm and its partners are considered the same entity.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: Lalu, Bhulu & Nilu. Lalu manages the firm. Bhulu & Nilu are bankrupt. Q: What type is Lalu?
mcqData.explanation:
A partner who manages and has unlimited liability is a General/Active partner.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) How will the firm dissolve since 2 out of 3 are bankrupt?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 41 states a firm MUST dissolve if all but one partner become insolvent.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: Partners must be- (i. Adult, ii. Educated, iii. Of sound mind)
mcqData.explanation:
Legal capacity requires adulthood and sanity, not a specific education level.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Max members in a banking partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
Banking partnership limit is 10.

Correct Answer: 10

mcqData.text: Stem: Jamil & Shamim invest 2.5L and 5L based on mutual understanding. Q: Business type?
mcqData.explanation:
Ownership shared by 2 people via contract is a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Profit is 1.2L. No ratio in deed. Q: Jamil's share?
mcqData.explanation:
Silent Deed = Equal Profit sharing. 1,20,000 / 2 = 60,000.

Correct Answer: 60,000

mcqData.text: Essential elements: (i. Contract, ii. Multiple members, iii. Registration)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration is not a mandatory legal requirement for existence.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Who allows the firm to use their name/reputation?
mcqData.explanation:
Nominal (Nammatro) partners lend their name for a fee or goodwill.

Correct Answer: Nominal

mcqData.text: What plays the most role in smooth management?
mcqData.explanation:
While contract is the basis, Trust (Saddbishshash) ensures smooth running.

Correct Answer: Mutual trust & confidence

mcqData.text: Stem: 3 friends form a firm to build a building. After profit, they decide to continue. Q: First firm type?
mcqData.explanation:
Formed for a specific task (building construction) is a Specific (Bishes) Partnership.

Correct Answer: Specific

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) What is the liability of the partners in the continued firm?
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership liability is always unlimited (Oshim) unless registered as 'Limited'.

Correct Answer: Unlimited

mcqData.text: Max members in a banking partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Banking limit is 10.

Correct Answer: 10

mcqData.text: A Limited partnership is one where-
mcqData.explanation:
Limited partnership must have at least one partner with limited liability.

Correct Answer: At least 1 limited

mcqData.text: Stem: Shamshul, Morsheda, Najmun & Badrun. Shamshul does not participate in mgmt. Q: Shamshul is?
mcqData.explanation:
Investing capital but not managing makes one a Passive (Nishkriyo) partner.

Correct Answer: Passive/Sleeping

mcqData.text: Stem: 4 partners. 3 invest 3L each, 1 is an engineer with no capital. No profit ratio in deed. Profit: 6L. Q: How much will Badrun get?
mcqData.explanation:
Silent Deed = Equal Profit sharing. 6,00,000 / 4 = 1,50,000.

Correct Answer: 1.5 Lakh

mcqData.text: Which section of the 1932 Act defines "Dissolution"?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 39 defines the dissolution of a firm (Bilopsadhon).

Correct Answer: 39

mcqData.text: Stem: Abid, Akash, Akib are partners. Friend Shohel is taken for his reputation/conduct with equal profit. Q: Shohel's type?
mcqData.explanation:
Taking a partner for name/reputation usage defines a Nominal (Nammatro) partner.

Correct Answer: Nominal

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Net profit 44,000. Q: Shohel's share?
mcqData.explanation:
Equal profit sharing for 4 friends: 44,000 / 4 = 11,000.

Correct Answer: 11,000

mcqData.text: Who is considered a "Real/True" partner?
mcqData.explanation:
Limited partners are real partners, whereas others like 'By Conduct' are only liable by law.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Which business is most risky regarding liability?
mcqData.explanation:
Partners have unlimited liability, making it the most risky among options.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Who is eligible to be a partner?
mcqData.explanation:
A sane adult housewife is legally capable of entering a contract.

Correct Answer: Housewife

mcqData.text: Stem: Johny, Rony, Sabbir (1L, 1.4L, 2L). They agree to share profit based on capital ratio. Q: Business type?
mcqData.explanation:
Multiple owners sharing profit/loss via agreement is a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Profit: 44,000. Q: Sabbir's share?
mcqData.explanation:
Capital ratio = 10:14:20 or 5:7:10. Total parts = 22. Sabbir gets (44,000 / 22) * 10 = 20,000.

Correct Answer: 20,000

mcqData.text: Stem: X, Y, Z (25k, 35k, 50k). Profit sharing by capital ratio. Q: Z's share if profit is 22,000?
mcqData.explanation:
Ratio = 25:35:50 or 5:7:10. Total = 22. Z gets (22,000 / 22) * 10 = 10,000.

Correct Answer: 10,000

mcqData.text: What is the full form of SME?
mcqData.explanation:
Small and Medium Enterprise is the correct term.

Correct Answer: Small & Medium Enterprise

mcqData.text: Which partner can claim profit from the business?
mcqData.explanation:
Limited partners have a right to profit; Quasi-partners only get interest on their loan.

Correct Answer: Limited

mcqData.text: Which section covers 'Dissolution by Notice'?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 43 covers dissolution of partnership at will by notice.

Correct Answer: 43

mcqData.text: Stem: Arita, Adiba (invest + mgmt), Jumaina (invest only, no time). Q: Jumaina is?
mcqData.explanation:
Investing capital but not participating in management defines a Sleeping (Supto) partner.

Correct Answer: Sleeping/Dormant

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) In which ratio will profit be shared (since ratio is not in deed)?
mcqData.explanation:
Silent Deed = Equal Profit (1:1:1).

Correct Answer: 1 : 1 : 1

mcqData.text: Max members in a banking partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
Banking limit is 10.

Correct Answer: 10

mcqData.text: Essential elements: (i. Multiple members, ii. Contractual, iii. Registration)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration is not a mandatory legal essential.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Which section defines "Partnership at Will"?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 7 defines 'Oichhik' partnership.

Correct Answer: 7

mcqData.text: Stem: A & B start a 5-year firm. After 5 years, they continue. Q: What type is it now?
mcqData.explanation:
Continuing a specific-term firm after its term expires makes it "Partnership at Will".

Correct Answer: Optional/At Will

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) They have a dispute of 35k with a debtor. Q: How can they recover? (i. Court, ii. Trade Assoc, iii. Negotiation)
mcqData.explanation:
Unregistered firms cannot sue for >100 Taka in court; they must use negotiation/arbitration.

Correct Answer: ii & iii

mcqData.text: What works as the guideline/director for managing a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
The "Partnership Deed" (Chuktipotro) contains all rules for operations.

Correct Answer: Partnership Deed

mcqData.text: Stem: Erika & 4 friends rent stalls for 2 months for a Trade Fair. Erika didn't participate in mgmt. Q: Business type?
mcqData.explanation:
Formed for a specific location (Trade Fair) or duration is a Special/Specific (Bishes) Partnership.

Correct Answer: Special

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Profit is 5L. Ratio not set. Erika wants equal share. Q: Erika's share?
mcqData.explanation:
Erika & 4 friends = 5 people. Silent ratio means equal sharing (1/5 each).

Correct Answer: One-fifth

mcqData.text: What is the core basis of a partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
Contract (Chukti) is the legal soul of partnership.

Correct Answer: Contract

mcqData.text: Stem: K, Kh, G start a firm. No profit ratio in agreement. Q: If the agreement is written, it's called?
mcqData.explanation:
A written partnership agreement is specifically called a "Partnership Deed" (Chuktipotro).

Correct Answer: Partnership Deed

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) A debtor owes 40k but firm can't act. Q: Steps needed to avoid this? (i. Written deed, ii. Registration, iii. Appointing arbitrator)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration is required to sue a third party for debts over 100 Taka.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Similarity between Sole Proprietorship and Partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
In both, owners have unlimited (Oshim) liability for business debts.

Correct Answer: Unlimited Liability

mcqData.text: Stem: Rajib, Liton, Masud. Masud bankrupt. Firm dissolves. Only Rajib's personal property seized. Q: Rajib is?
mcqData.explanation:
Active/General partners have unlimited liability extending to personal assets.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) How will the firm dissolve since Masud is bankrupt?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 41: Dissolution is compulsory if any partner becomes insolvent.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: Stem: Suha & Ayana (3L & 4L capital) start a business. Q: Organization type?
mcqData.explanation:
Multiple owners (2) with capital investment is a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: P, Q, R admit Mr. S as a Limited Partner. S is happy. Q: Main reason for S's happiness?
mcqData.explanation:
Limited partners only risk their invested capital, not personal assets.

Correct Answer: Limited liability

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Reason for taking S? (i. New capital, ii. No mgmt complexity, iii. Less profit)
mcqData.explanation:
Limited partners provide capital but cannot interfere in management.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Partner who retires but doesn't withdraw capital (leaves it as a loan)?
mcqData.explanation:
A Quasi-partner (Apato-drishtite) leaves their capital as a loan in the firm.

Correct Answer: Quasi-partner

mcqData.text: Businesses with Unlimited Liability: (i. Sole, ii. Cooperative, iii. Partnership)
mcqData.explanation:
Cooperatives have limited liability; Sole and Partnership are unlimited.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Stem: Fahim manages the firm but doesn't provide capital. Q: Fahim is?
mcqData.explanation:
Worker (Kormi) partners provide labor/skill instead of money.

Correct Answer: Worker

mcqData.text: Roni, Joni, Soni. Soni provides capital but doesn't manage. Q: Soni's type?
mcqData.explanation:
Investing capital without management makes one a Sleeping partner.

Correct Answer: Sleeping/Passive

mcqData.text: Stem: Jahid, Shofiq, Anwar (Partners). Q: Core basis of their business?
mcqData.explanation:
(Repeat Concept) Contract (Chukti) is the foundation.

Correct Answer: Contract

mcqData.text: Stem: (Cont. from above) Best way to solve long-term issues?
mcqData.explanation:
A clear "Partnership Deed" is the best way to prevent and solve disputes.

Correct Answer: Deed/Contract

mcqData.text: Stem: Maruf (Sales Rep) introduces himself as a partner. Q: Maruf is?
mcqData.explanation:
A person who acts or speaks as if they are a partner is "By Conduct" (Achorone Onumito).

Correct Answer: By Conduct

mcqData.text: Stem: Siam starts a shop alone. Later takes friend Nirjon. Q: Initially, what was it?
mcqData.explanation:
A business owned and run by one person is Sole Proprietorship (Ek-malikana).

Correct Answer: Sole Proprietorship

mcqData.text: Stem: Siam starts a shop with 1L capital. Later takes Nirjon with 60k. Profit: 20,000. Q: How much profit will Nirjon receive?
mcqData.explanation:
Since the stem doesn't mention a profit-sharing ratio, profit is shared EQUALLY (1:1). 20,000 / 2 = 10,000.

Correct Answer: 10,000

mcqData.text: A partner who doesn't invest capital but actively uses their expertise/intellect in business?
mcqData.explanation:
A Worker Partner (Kormi) provides labor or specialized skill/intellect instead of money.

Correct Answer: Worker

mcqData.text: Minimum number of members in a general partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
According to the 1932 Act, a partnership must have at least 2 members.

Correct Answer: 2

mcqData.text: How many ways can a partnership business be dissolved?
mcqData.explanation:
According to Sections 40-44 of the 1932 Act, there are 5 methods of dissolution.

Correct Answer: 5

mcqData.text: Even if children run the business after a person's death, it is not called a partnership. Why?
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership is based on a contract (Section 5), not on status or inheritance.

Correct Answer: Absence of contractual relationship

mcqData.text: Stem: Tina, Mila, and Rubi start a business. Capitals: 1L, 1.4L, and 2L. Q: What type of business is this?
mcqData.explanation:
A business formed by multiple people based on an agreement is a partnership.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) If the profit is 75,000, how much profit will Rubi receive?
mcqData.explanation:
If the ratio is not mentioned in the deed, profit is shared equally (75,000 ÷ 3).

Correct Answer: 25,000

mcqData.text: If it's impossible to maintain the minimum of 2 members, what type of dissolution occurs?
mcqData.explanation:
Under Section 41, if members drop to 1, the business must be dissolved compulsorily.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: Which subject is mentioned in Section 58 of the Partnership Act of 1932?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 58 describes the procedure for registration of a firm.

Correct Answer: Registration

mcqData.text: Stem: Sohag does not participate in management. Q: What type of partner is Sohag?
mcqData.explanation:
A partner who provides capital but does not manage is a Sleeping/Passive partner.

Correct Answer: Sleeping

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) How will the firm dissolve due to Sohag's death?
mcqData.explanation:
Under Section 42(c), the death of a partner causes dissolution as a special event.

Correct Answer: Subject to special event

mcqData.text: Which Partnership Act is currently in force in Bangladesh?
mcqData.explanation:
The Partnership Act of 1932 is the governing law in Bangladesh.

Correct Answer: 1932

mcqData.text: Which partnership is dissolved by a simple written notice?
mcqData.explanation:
Section 43 states a Partnership at Will can be dissolved by any partner via notice.

Correct Answer: Optional/At Will

mcqData.text: Essential elements: (i. Multiple members, ii. Registration, iii. Contractual relationship)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration is not a mandatory or essential element for the existence of a firm.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: Reasons for Compulsory Dissolution: (i. Illegal act, ii. Insolvency, iii. Death)
mcqData.explanation:
Section 41: Death is a special event, not a cause for compulsory dissolution.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Reasons for creating a partnership: (i. Capital shortage, ii. Personal limits, iii. Large scale benefits)
mcqData.explanation:
Partnership is formed to overcome the limitations of sole proprietorship.

Correct Answer: i, ii & iii

mcqData.text: Due to which of the following can a case NOT be filed in the name of the firm?
mcqData.explanation:
Without a separate legal entity, a firm cannot sue or be sued in its own name.

Correct Answer: Lack of separate entity

mcqData.text: In which country did Limited Partnership originate?
mcqData.explanation:
Limited partnerships (Commenda) originated in medieval Italy.

Correct Answer: Italy

mcqData.text: Stem: Shamshun did not participate in management. Q: What type of partner is Shamshun?
mcqData.explanation:
Because she does not manage, she is a Passive/Sleeping partner.

Correct Answer: Passive

mcqData.text: Stem: Badrun (Engineer) gave no capital. Profit is 6L. Q: How much profit will Badrun get?
mcqData.explanation:
With 4 partners and no deed ratio, profit is divided equally (6 ÷ 4).

Correct Answer: 1.5 Lakh

mcqData.text: Dissolution by the Court can occur when-
mcqData.explanation:
Section 44 allows the court to dissolve a firm for persistent misconduct.

Correct Answer: Persistent breach of contract

mcqData.text: Stem: Jahed & 3 friends start a firm for a 2-year term. Q: What type of partnership is this?
mcqData.explanation:
A partnership formed for a fixed term (2 years) is a Particular/Specific Time partnership.

Correct Answer: Specific period

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) Debt: 3,00,000. 4 partners in total. Q: How much debt should Jahed pay?
mcqData.explanation:
Silence in the deed means equal sharing. 3,00,000 ÷ 4 = 75,000 (Option B is the closest round figure).

Correct Answer: 1,00,000

mcqData.text: Stem: Shimul, Limon, and Abid. Shimul manages the firm. Limon and Abid are declared bankrupt by court. Q: What type of partner is Shimul?
mcqData.explanation:
A partner who manages and has unlimited liability is a General/Active partner.

Correct Answer: General

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) How will the business be dissolved since Limon and Abid are bankrupt?
mcqData.explanation:
Under Section 41, if all but one partner become insolvent, dissolution is compulsory.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: Registration of a partnership business is—
mcqData.explanation:
According to the 1932 Act, registration is optional, though non-registration has disadvantages.

Correct Answer: Optional

mcqData.text: Compulsory dissolution happens when— (i. Business is illegal, ii. All but one bankrupt, iii. Death of a member)
mcqData.explanation:
Section 41 defines compulsory dissolution; death (Sec 42) is a special event.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Who is eligible to be a partner?
mcqData.explanation:
A housewife who is an adult and of sound mind is legally capable of entering a contract.

Correct Answer: Housewife

mcqData.text: What is the maximum number of members in a general partnership?
mcqData.explanation:
The maximum limit for a general partnership is 20 members.

Correct Answer: 20

mcqData.text: Who does NOT have to carry unlimited liability?
mcqData.explanation:
A limited partner's liability is restricted to their invested capital.

Correct Answer: Limited Partner

mcqData.text: Stem: X, Y, Z were partners. Y died. X and Z admitted Y's minor son. Q: What is Y's son called?
mcqData.explanation:
Under the law, a minor admitted to the benefits of a firm is treated as a limited partner.

Correct Answer: Limited Partner

mcqData.text: Stem: (Part of above) The purpose of keeping the minor as a partner is— (i. Benefits, ii. Continuity, iii. Development)
mcqData.explanation:
Minors are primarily admitted for their benefit and to train them as future businessmen.

Correct Answer: i & iii

mcqData.text: If 2 out of 3 partners are declared bankrupt, the dissolution will be—

(Repeat Concept) If all but one are insolvent, it must close under Section 41.

Correct Answer: Compulsory

mcqData.text: Mutual Agency (Representation) exists in—
mcqData.explanation:
Mutual Agency is a core feature where each partner is both principal and agent.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Partnership matters include: (i. Mandatory capital, ii. Written/Registered deed, iii. Mutual trust)
mcqData.explanation:
Capital isn't always mandatory (Worker partner) and deeds can be oral; Trust is essential.

Correct Answer: iii

mcqData.text: What is the relationship between partners called?
mcqData.explanation:
"Partnership" is the name of the relationship between persons who have agreed to share profits.

Correct Answer: Partnership

mcqData.text: Which feature makes a partner liable for the acts of another partner?
mcqData.explanation:
Due to Mutual Agency, an act done by one partner binds the whole firm.

Correct Answer: Mutual Agency

mcqData.text: Essential elements: (i. Multiple members, ii. Contractual rel., iii. Registration)
mcqData.explanation:
Registration (iii) is a formality, not an "essential element" for formation.

Correct Answer: i & ii

mcqData.text: Akib and friends signed a contract for 3 years. What type of partnership is this?
mcqData.explanation:
A partnership formed for a fixed duration (3 years) is a Particular Partnership.

Correct Answer: Particular/Specific

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: Who is a nominal partner?
shortCQData.answer: A nominal partner is someone who allows a firm to use their name and reputation but does not invest capital or participate in management, though they remain liable to third parties.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: "Agreement is the foundation of partnership"—Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. A partnership is born out of a contract, not status or birth.

2. The rights, duties, and profit-sharing ratios are all determined by this document, making it the legal basis of the relationship.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership deed?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership deed is a written legal document containing the terms and conditions agreed upon by the partners for running the business.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is meant by compulsory dissolution? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. It refers to the legal closure of a firm when all partners (or all but one) become insolvent, or if the business becomes illegal.

2. In such cases, the firm must be closed by law regardless of the partners' wishes.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: Who is a partner?
shortCQData.answer: A partner is a person who has entered into an agreement with others to carry on a legal business and share its profits and losses.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is meant by a partnership agreement?
shortCQData.answer:
1. It is the mutual understanding or contract (written or oral) that defines the rules, profit ratios, and roles of each partner.

2. It serves as the governing law for the internal affairs of the firm.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership deed?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership deed is a written legal document that contains the terms, conditions, and rules agreed upon by the partners for conducting their business.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Why is it better for a partnership agreement to be in writing? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. A written agreement acts as documentary evidence in case of future disputes among partners.

2. It provides clear legal guidance regarding profit sharing, roles, and dissolution, making it easier to resolve conflicts through the court.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a general partnership business?
shortCQData.answer: A general partnership is a business where all partners have unlimited liability and are collectively responsible for the firm's debts and management.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: The liability of a partnership is unlimited—Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. It means partners are personally responsible for the firm's debts.

2. If the firm's assets are insufficient to pay creditors, the partners' private properties can be seized and sold to settle the claims.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is the maximum number of members in a banking partnership?
shortCQData.answer: The maximum number of members in a banking partnership business is 10.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What kind of relationship do partners share? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. Partners share a relationship of Mutual Agency.

2. This means every partner is both a principal (for themselves) and an agent (for other partners), where the actions of one partner bind the entire firm.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: Who are quasi-partners?
shortCQData.answer: Quasi-partners are retired partners who leave their capital in the firm as a loan instead of withdrawing it; they receive interest but do not participate in management.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is meant by bonus shares?
shortCQData.answer:
1. Bonus shares are free shares issued by a company to its existing shareholders out of its accumulated profits or reserves.

2. It increases the total number of shares without requiring any additional cash payment from the shareholders.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: Who is a working partner?
shortCQData.answer: A working partner is a member of a partnership who does not invest capital but provides active labor and professional skills to manage the business.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Why is the agreement called the foundation of partnership?
shortCQData.answer:
1. Partnership is a contractual relationship, not a result of birth or status.

2. The agreement dictates all rights, duties, and future actions, making it the legal basis of the firm's existence.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership business?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership business is a relationship between persons who have agreed to share the profits of a legal business carried on by all or any of them acting for all.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Why is the registration of a partnership firm mandatory?
shortCQData.answer: 1. While not legally compulsory by the Act, registration is practically mandatory to gain legal rights, such as the ability to sue third parties or other partners in court.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: Who is a nominal partner?
shortCQData.answer: A nominal partner is someone who lends their name and reputation to a firm but does not invest capital or participate in management.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Why does a partnership business have no separate legal entity? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. It lacks a separate legal entity because the business and the owners are considered one in the eyes of the law.

2. Consequently, the firm cannot sue or be sued in its own name, and partners have unlimited liability.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership business?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership is a legal business relationship based on an agreement between two or more persons to share profits and losses.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Can a minor be a partner? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. A minor cannot be a full partner because they lack the legal capacity to contract.

2. However, under Section 30, they can be admitted to the benefits of an existing partnership with the consent of all partners.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is business?
shortCQData.answer: Business refers to any legal economic activity (production, distribution, or services) conducted with the primary goal of earning profit.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: "Agreement is the foundation of partnership"—Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. Partnership is created by a contract, not by status.

2. The agreement defines the rights, roles, and profit-sharing ratios, serving as the ultimate guide for the firm's operations.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a contract/agreement?
shortCQData.answer: An agreement is a promise or a set of promises, forming the consideration for each party, which is enforceable by law. In partnership, it is the foundation of the business relationship.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is a 'sleeping partner'? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. A sleeping (dormant) partner is a member who contributes capital and shares in profits/losses but does not take an active part in the daily management of the firm.

2. Their liability remains unlimited, but they are not known to the public as active members.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership deed?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership deed is the written document containing the terms, conditions, and rules agreed upon by the partners for the conduct of the business.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: "Registered partnership business is better"—Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. Registration provides legal protection; a registered firm can sue third parties or other partners to recover dues or settle disputes.

2. Unregistered firms face legal disabilities, making registration a "better" choice for security.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a National Cooperative Society?
shortCQData.answer: A National Cooperative Society is the apex body of the cooperative movement in a country, formed by the federation of central cooperative societies to promote and coordinate cooperative activities.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is meant by a nominal partner? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. A nominal partner allows the firm to use their name and reputation without investing capital or participating in management.

2. They do not usually share in profits but are liable to third parties who provide credit based on their name.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership deed?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership deed is a written legal document that contains the terms, conditions, and rules agreed upon by the partners for the conduct and management of the business.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Can a minor be a partner? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. Generally, a minor cannot be a partner because they lack the legal capacity to contract.

2. However, under Section 30 of the Partnership Act 1932, a minor may be admitted to the benefits of an existing partnership with the consent of all partners.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a nominal partner?
shortCQData.answer: A nominal partner is someone who allows a firm to use their name and reputation but does not invest capital or participate in management, though they remain liable to third parties.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: "Agreement is the foundation of partnership"—Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. A partnership is created by a contract, not by status, birth, or inheritance.

2. The agreement (deed) determines the rights, duties, and profit ratios of the partners, serving as the legal roadmap for the firm.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: Who is a limited partner?
shortCQData.answer: A limited partner is a member whose liability is restricted only to the amount of capital they have invested in the firm, and they generally cannot participate in management.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is meant by a partner by holding out (Estoppel)?
shortCQData.answer:
1. It refers to a person who, through their words or behavior, leads others to believe they are a partner in a firm.

2. Legally, they are held liable for the firm's debts to third parties who relied on that belief.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is an agreement?
shortCQData.answer: An agreement is a promise or set of promises forming consideration for each other, which creates a legal obligation when enforceable by law.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Who is a sleeping partner? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. A sleeping partner is an owner who invests capital and shares profits/losses but does not participate in the daily management of the firm.

2. They are not known to the outside world as partners, but their liability remains unlimited.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a sleeping partner?
shortCQData.answer: A sleeping partner is one who contributes capital and shares profits but stays away from the active management of the firm.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Why does a partnership business dissolve easily?
shortCQData.answer:
1. It lacks a separate legal entity and perpetual succession.

2. It relies on the mutual trust of partners; death, insanity, or a simple notice (in partnership at will) can lead to its closure.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is partnership?
shortCQData.answer: Partnership is the relation between persons who have agreed to share the profits of a business carried on by all or any of them acting for all.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Why is a registered agreement important?
shortCQData.answer:
1. It serves as strong documentary evidence in court during disputes.

2. Registration grants the firm legal rights to sue third parties and settle internal conflicts officially.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a limited partner?
shortCQData.answer: A limited partner is one whose liability is restricted to the amount of capital they have invested in the firm and who usually has no management rights.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Why is a registered partnership better? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. It provides the firm with the legal standing to sue third parties for debts exceeding 100 BDT.

2. It protects partners' rights against each other and ensures smoother legal transitions.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is minimum capital?
shortCQData.answer: Minimum capital is the initial amount of money required by law or a company's bylaws to start a business or for a public limited company to commence its operations.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Can government officials be members of a partnership? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. Generally, government officials cannot be members of a partnership business.

2. Service rules usually prohibit them from engaging in trade or commercial activities that might conflict with their official duties or public interest.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a limited partnership?
shortCQData.answer: A limited partnership is a firm where the liability of at least one partner is limited to their invested capital, while at least one other partner has unlimited liability.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Why is a registered partnership better?
shortCQData.answer:
1. Registration provides the firm with legal rights to sue third parties to recover debts exceeding 100 BDT.

2. It also protects the rights of partners against each other in case of internal disputes.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership at will?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership at will is a business where no fixed duration or specific venture is mentioned in the agreement, allowing any partner to dissolve it by giving a notice.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Explain the consequences of an unregistered partnership.
shortCQData.answer:
1. An unregistered firm cannot sue third parties in court for dues over 100 BDT.

2. Partners cannot sue each other or the firm to enforce rights mentioned in the agreement.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partner by holding out (estoppel)?
shortCQData.answer: A partner by holding out is a person who, through their words or conduct, represents themselves as a partner (or allows others to do so), making them liable to third parties who give credit to the firm based on that belief.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: "Agreement is the foundation of partnership"—Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. Partnership is a contractual relationship, not a result of status or birth.

2. The agreement (deed) dictates the rights, duties, and future actions of partners, serving as the legal roadmap for the firm's existence.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership deed?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership deed is a written document containing the terms, conditions, and rules agreed upon by the partners for the conduct and management of the business.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is meant by partnership at will?
shortCQData.answer:
1. A partnership at will is a business where no fixed duration or specific venture is mentioned.

2. It can be dissolved at any time by any partner giving a written notice to the others.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: Who is a partner by estoppel?
shortCQData.answer: A partner by estoppel is a person who, by their behavior or words, leads others to believe they are a partner, even though they have no actual agreement or share in the firm.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is meant by a partnership deed? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. It is the written contract that governs the internal management of the firm.

2. It prevents disputes by clarifying profit ratios, capital amounts, and roles of each partner.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partner by holding out (estoppel)?
shortCQData.answer: A partner by holding out is a person who, through words or conduct, represents themselves as a partner, making them liable to third parties who give credit to the firm based on that belief.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: "What is the foundation of partnership?"—Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. The foundation of partnership is the Agreement (Contract).

2. It is not created by status or birth, but by a mutual understanding that defines the rights and duties of all members.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a contract/agreement?
shortCQData.answer: An agreement is a promise or a set of promises forming the consideration for each party, which is enforceable by law.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is a limited partnership? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. It is a partnership where the liability of at least one member is limited to their investment.

2. Such firms must be registered by law to enjoy this status.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: Who is a working partner?
shortCQData.answer: A working partner is one who contributes no capital but provides active labor and management skills to the firm.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Can a minor be a partner? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. A minor cannot be a full partner as they cannot legally contract.

2. However, they can be admitted to the benefits of a firm with everyone's consent.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership deed?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership deed is the written legal document containing the terms, conditions, and rules agreed upon by partners for the management of their business.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is a limited partnership? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. It is a partnership where at least one partner has limited liability (up to their investment), while at least one other partner has unlimited liability.

2. Such firms must be registered under the 1932 Act to be legally recognized.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership deed?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership deed is a written contract that serves as the governing law for the internal affairs of a partnership firm.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Can a minor be a partner? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. A minor cannot be a full partner as they lack the legal capacity to contract.

2. However, they can be admitted to the benefits of the firm with the consent of all partners.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is the foundation of partnership?
shortCQData.answer: The foundation of a partnership business is the Agreement (Contract).

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Can a minor be a partner in a partnership business?
shortCQData.answer: 1. A minor cannot be a partner in the legal sense, but can be admitted to the benefits of the firm by mutual consent.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is dissolution of partnership?
shortCQData.answer: Dissolution of partnership refers to the termination of the legal relationship and contractual agreement between the partners, leading to the closure of the firm's operations.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is the nature of partnership registration? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. Registration is voluntary, not compulsory, under the Partnership Act 1932.

2. However, an unregistered firm faces several legal disabilities, making registration highly recommended for legal protection.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is unlimited liability?
shortCQData.answer: Unlimited liability means the owners are personally responsible for all business debts; if business assets are insufficient, their private property can be seized to pay creditors.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Can a minor be a partner? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. A minor cannot be a full partner because they lack the legal capacity to contract.

2. However, under Section 30, they can be admitted to the benefits of a firm with the consent of all partners.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a share?
shortCQData.answer: A share is the smallest unit into which the total capital of a company is divided.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Why is the Memorandum of Association called the main document of a company?
shortCQData.answer:
1. It is the constitution of the company that defines its name, objects, and scope of power.

2. A company cannot perform any act that falls outside the boundaries set by its Memorandum.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: Who is a working partner?
shortCQData.answer: A working partner is a person who contributes no capital to the firm but provides active labor and management expertise, sharing in the profits as per the agreement.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is meant by the unlimited liability of partners?
shortCQData.answer:
1. It means partners are personally liable for the firm's debts beyond their invested capital.

2. If the firm's assets are insufficient, creditors can claim the partners' private properties to recover the money.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership business?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership is a business relationship between people who have agreed to share the profits of a business carried on by all or any of them acting for all.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What do you mean by a sleeping partner?
shortCQData.answer:
1. A sleeping partner contributes capital and shares profits/losses but does not participate in the daily management of the firm.

2. Their existence is often unknown to the general public.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a limited partnership?
shortCQData.answer: A limited partnership is a firm where at least one partner has liability limited to their capital investment, while at least one other partner has unlimited liability.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Can a minor be a partner? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. A minor cannot be a full partner as they cannot legally sign a contract.

2. However, they can be admitted to the benefits of the business with the consent of all existing partners.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: Define partnership business.
shortCQData.answer: A partnership is a relationship between persons who have agreed to share the profits of a business carried on by all or any of them acting for all.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is a partner by holding out (estoppel)? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. It refers to a person who, through their words or behavior, leads others to believe they are a partner.

2. Legally, they are held liable for the firm's debts to third parties who relied on that belief, even if they aren't actual partners.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a National Cooperative Society?
shortCQData.answer: It is the highest-level cooperative body in a country, formed by the federation of central cooperative societies to lead and coordinate the cooperative movement.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What do you mean by minimum subscription? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. It is the minimum amount of capital that a public limited company must raise through share sales before it can legally commence business.

2. This ensures the company has enough funds for its initial operational needs.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership deed?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership deed is the written document containing the terms, conditions, and rules agreed upon by the partners for running the business.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is meant by the indirect benefit of unlimited liability?
shortCQData.answer:
1. Unlimited liability makes partners more cautious and responsible, as their personal assets are at risk.

2. This builds higher trust among creditors and suppliers, making it easier for the firm to obtain loans and credit.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is cultural environment?
shortCQData.answer: Cultural environment consists of the values, beliefs, customs, and lifestyles of a society that influence the demand and operations of a business.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Why are direct services non-storable? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. Direct services (like a haircut or a doctor's checkup) are produced and consumed simultaneously.

2. Since they are intangible and require the presence of the service provider, they cannot be manufactured in advance and stored for future use.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a sole proprietorship?
shortCQData.answer: A sole proprietorship is a business owned, managed, and controlled by a single individual who bears all risks and enjoys all profits.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Why is sole proprietorship popular? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. It is popular due to its ease of formation, minimal legal formalities, and the owner's absolute control over decision-making.

2. It also allows for direct customer relations and secrecy.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership business?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership is a legal relationship based on an agreement between persons to share profits and losses of a business.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is meant by the unlimited liability of partners?
shortCQData.answer:
1. It means partners are personally liable for the firm's debts.

2. If the firm's assets are insufficient, their private property can be seized and sold to pay off the firm's creditors.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a limited partnership?
shortCQData.answer: A limited partnership is a firm where the liability of at least one partner is limited to their invested capital, while at least one other partner has unlimited liability.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Why is a registered partnership better?
shortCQData.answer:
1. Registration provides the firm with legal rights to sue third parties to recover debts exceeding 100 BDT.

2. It also protects the rights of partners against each other in case of internal disputes.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is unlimited liability?
shortCQData.answer: Unlimited liability means the owners are personally responsible for all business debts; if business assets are insufficient, their private property can be seized to pay creditors.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: 'Agreement is the foundation of partnership'—Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. Partnership is a contractual relationship, not a result of status or birth.

2. The agreement (deed) dictates the rights, duties, and future actions of partners, serving as the legal roadmap for the firm's existence.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is bankruptcy?
shortCQData.answer: Bankruptcy (Insolvency) is a legal status of a person or entity that cannot repay the debts they owe to creditors.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is meant by the indirect benefit of unlimited risk? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. Unlimited risk makes partners more cautious and responsible, as their personal assets are at stake.

2. This builds higher trust among creditors and suppliers, making it easier for the firm to obtain loans.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: Who is a sleeping partner?
shortCQData.answer: A sleeping (dormant) partner is an owner who contributes capital and shares in profits/losses but does not participate in the active management of the business.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Why is a registered agreement important?
shortCQData.answer:
1. It acts as conclusive evidence in court to resolve internal disputes.

2. It grants the firm the legal right to sue third parties for debts exceeding 100 BDT.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: Who is a quasi-partner?
shortCQData.answer: A quasi-partner is a retiring partner who leaves their capital in the firm as a loan instead of withdrawing it, receiving interest instead of a profit share.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is meant by partnership at will?
shortCQData.answer:
1. It is a partnership where no fixed duration or specific venture is mentioned in the agreement.

2. It can be dissolved at any time if any partner gives a written notice to the others.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership deed?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership deed is a written document containing the rules and terms agreed upon by the partners for the internal management of the firm.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Can a minor be a partner? Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. A minor cannot be a full partner as they lack the legal capacity to contract.

2. However, they can be admitted to the benefits of the business with the consent of all existing partners.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a statutory duty?
shortCQData.answer: A statutory duty is a legal obligation imposed by law or a specific act (like the Companies Act) that must be performed by the concerned parties.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: "Partnership arises from contract, not from status"—Explain.
shortCQData.answer:
1. Unlike a Hindu Undivided Family business where membership is by birth (status), partnership requires a formal agreement.

2. It is a voluntary contractual relationship between competent persons to carry out business for profit.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is partnership?
shortCQData.answer: Partnership is the relation between persons who have agreed to share the profits of a business carried on by all or any of them acting for all.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: What is meant by the indirect benefit of unlimited liability?
shortCQData.answer:
1. It makes partners more cautious and responsible in decision-making to avoid personal loss.

2. This increases the firm's creditworthiness, as creditors know they can recover money from the partners' personal assets.

shortCQData.text: (a) Knowledge: What is a partnership deed?
shortCQData.answer: A partnership deed is a written legal document that contains the terms, conditions, and rules agreed upon by the partners for the conduct and management of the business.

shortCQData.text: (b) Understanding: Explain the indirect benefit of unlimited liability in partnership.
shortCQData.answer:
1. It increases the firm's creditworthiness because creditors know they can recover their money from the partners' personal assets if the business fails.

2. It makes partners more cautious and responsible in management, as they are personally at risk for any financial loss.

fullCQData.stem: Ankan Traders' is a partnership firm. Their contract stated they would farm fish for seven years by leasing a pond. After seven years, they are unable to recover 50,000 BDT from Mr. Rahim for fish sales. They failed to seek legal help from the court to recover the amount.

questions.label: a
questions.text: (a) Knowledge: Who is a nominal partner?
questions.answer: A nominal partner is someone who allows a firm to use their name and reputation but does not invest capital or participate in management, though they remain liable to third parties.

questions.label: b
questions.text: (b) Understanding: "Agreement is the foundation of partnership"—Explain.
questions.answer:
1. A partnership is born out of a contract, not status or birth.
