To achieve this, BPC designs, builds, and operates its own commercial hotels, luxury motels, tourist information centers, duty-free shops, and picnic spots, ensuring affordable and secure hospitality while actively generating revenue for the state exchequer.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which state-owned institution is mentioned first in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Paragraph 1: Cognitive Identification

The state-owned institution mentioned first in the stem is the Bangladesh Post Office (Postal Department).

Paragraph 2: Conceptual Explanation

A departmental state-owned enterprise is a public utility service run directly under the control, budget, and administrative framework of a specific government ministry. These entities are usually established to ensure nationwide traditional public service distribution where financial profit is secondary to the mandatory social communication framework, and they are protected by sovereign operational historical laws.

Paragraph 3: Contextual Application

The stem drops an explicit literary clue regarding a 'short story by Rabindranath' (referring to the classic story The Postmaster) and notes that this communication medium has survived uninterrupted since the British Raj. While it traditionally maintained a monopoly over sending personal mail, money orders, and documents, the contemporary boom of smart digital networks, mobile banking, and fast private courier agencies has deeply eroded its market share. Therefore, this ancient but surviving communication network is identified as the state-owned postal department.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Give your opinion on the necessity of improving the quality of customer service of the institution mentioned later in the stem.
questions.answer:
Paragraph 1: Direct Stance on Service Upgrade

Improving the customer service quality of the state transport institution mentioned later in the stem—Bangladesh Railway—is an absolute, urgent necessity to fulfill public welfare and optimize national asset efficiency.

Paragraph 2: Theoretical Imperative of Public Transport Safety

State-run transport organizations exist primarily to distribute high-volume, safe, and heavily subsidized mobility options to the general public. While their absolute state-backed monopoly shields them from being wiped out by private market competitors, an ongoing failure to provide basic customer care transforms a critical national asset into a massive fiscal drain on tax dollars.

Paragraph 3: Analyzing the Operational Deficiencies

The stem describes this secondary institution as a safe, cost-effective transport legacy from the British era whose organic public demand remains phenomenally high due to journey comfort. This description clearly points to Bangladesh Railway. However, despite its non-competitive monopoly and massive public reliance, it continues to suffer from poor schedule maintenance, black-market ticket hoarding, unhygienic conditions, and severe customer neglect. Because citizens have no alternative cheap rail option, they are forced to deal with low-quality service, causing deep public dissatisfaction.

Paragraph 4: Evaluative Conclusion

In conclusion, since Bangladesh Railway carries the structural weight of the nation's daily commute, modernizing its service delivery is critical. By automating ticket distribution to erase black markets, guaranteeing absolute train punctuality, and enhancing coach hygiene, the state can instantly convert public frustration into national satisfaction. Upgrading this customer experience is essential to justify state funding and drive the country's macroeconomic industrial connectivity forward.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Ahmed Ali is a government employee. When the prices of essential commodities rise significantly in the market, Mr. Ahmed Ali's organization arranges the sale of goods at lower prices. This benefits the public. This organization prioritizes public welfare without considering profit.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is business?
questions.answer: Business refers to any legal economic activity conducted with the primary objective of earning profit through the production and distribution of goods and services.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by a buying house?
questions.answer:
Paragraph 1: Definition of Buying House

A buying house acts as a specialized commercial intermediary or agency in the international trade sector, primarily serving as a bridge between foreign buyers (importers) and local garment manufacturers (exporters).

Paragraph 2: Operational Role and Functions

The key function of a buying house is to secure orders from overseas garment buyers and distribute those production orders among local readymade garment (RMG) factories. It handles critical tasks such as sample development, product quality control, price negotiation, production monitoring, and ensuring timely shipment compliance. It plays an indispensable role in boosting a nation's export earnings by streamlining the supply chain for international fashion markets.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What is the name of Mr. Ahmed Ali's organization? Explain.
questions.answer:
Paragraph 1: Cognitive Identification

The name of the state-owned organization where Mr. Ahmed Ali works is the Trading Corporation of Bangladesh (TCB).

Paragraph 2: Conceptual Explanation

A state-owned trading enterprise is a commercial statutory body established by the government to regulate domestic market prices, manage national buffer stocks, and ensure food security. Instead of focusing on profit maximization, its operational strategy revolves around market intervention—importing and selling daily essentials at subsidized rates to protect lower-income citizens from artificial inflation and hoarding during commodity market crises.

Paragraph 3: Contextual Application

The stem notes that Mr. Ahmed Ali works for a state organization that intervenes in the market precisely when the prices of daily consumer goods surge uncontrollably. By selling essential items below market rates, this entity directly shields vulnerable families and puts public welfare ahead of profit. In Bangladesh, this exact consumer-protection mandate is executed by the Trading Corporation of Bangladesh (TCB). Thus, it is clear that Mr. Ali is employed by TCB.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Should the activities of the organization described in the stem be expanded? Write with arguments.
questions.answer:
Paragraph 1: Direct Stance on Expansion

Yes, I strongly argue that the market operations and distribution infrastructure of the Trading Corporation of Bangladesh (TCB) should be heavily expanded across the nation.

Paragraph 2: Theoretical Imperative of Market Intervention

In a developing market economy, syndicates, middlemen, and speculative hoarders frequently trigger artificial price hikes of daily essentials, exploiting the general public. State-run trading organizations act as a vital counter-regulatory mechanism; by aggressively injecting low-cost supplies into the open market, they break monopolistic cartels and restore reasonable price equilibrium.

Paragraph 3: Analytical Justification Based on the Stem

As highlighted in the stem, TCB's structural intervention directly benefits the public by making essential food items affordable when inflation strikes. However, if these sub-market sales remain restricted only to metropolitan trucking points, a vast percentage of remote, rural, and grassroots working-class populations remain excluded from this safety net. Scaling up operations—by building permanent regional warehouses and introducing digital subsidized ration cards—will completely eliminate the leverage held by corrupt trade syndicates.

Paragraph 4: Evaluative Conclusion

In conclusion, expanding TCB's operational reach is a highly effective strategic policy for ensuring nationwide food security and social stability. Transforming it into a robust, widespread consumer distribution network will seamlessly protect the purchasing power of low-income citizens, fulfilling the state's constitutional commitment to public welfare and strengthening the country's macroeconomic resilience.

fullCQData.stem: The government wants to establish industries and business enterprises everywhere in the country. Thinking about the transportation of people and goods at affordable prices, the government has been operating public transport across the country. Although the public transport service under the Ministry of Communication is keen to serve the general public, it is not possible because the number of their buses and trucks is very small compared to the requirement.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is BRTC?
questions.answer: BRTC stands for Bangladesh Road Transport Corporation, which is a state-owned statutory public transport corporation of Bangladesh.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Which business has public welfare as its objective? Explain.
questions.answer:
Paragraph 1: Identification of State-Owned Enterprise

State-owned enterprise (SOE) is the business organization whose primary objective is public welfare rather than maximizing profit.

Paragraph 2: Explanation of Welfare Objective

Unlike private businesses that operate for individual gain, state-owned businesses are established, funded, and controlled by the government to provide essential public services like transport, utilities, and infrastructure at subsidized rates. By prioritizing equitable access over high margins, these organizations ensure that basic needs are met for all citizens, thereby fulfilling the state's social commitment to national development.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which objective of the government is reflected in setting up the industries described in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Paragraph 1: Identification of Goal

The government objective reflected here is 'Balanced Regional Industrialization' and 'Decentralization of Economic Activities'.

Paragraph 2: Conceptual Analysis

The government seeks to avoid industrial concentration in a few urban hubs by promoting the establishment of businesses in remote or underdeveloped regions. This strategy aims to leverage local resources, create widespread employment, and reduce the regional economic disparity that causes mass migration.

Paragraph 3: Contextual Application

The stem specifies that the government's aim is to establish industries 'everywhere' in the country. To support this objective, a reliable logistics and transport network is required. Therefore, the government operates affordable public transport to ferry workers and industrial goods, ensuring that the benefits of industrialization are evenly distributed across the nation.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Which objective of the government is reflected in setting up the industries described in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Paragraph 1: Stance on the Issue

While the government's objective of nationwide industrialization is strategic and necessary, the practical implementation is significantly hampered by the inadequate fleet of the state-owned transport corporation.

Paragraph 2: Theoretical Imperative of Capacity

For a state-owned welfare enterprise to succeed, it must possess sufficient operational capacity to meet the baseline public demand. If an organization cannot scale its services, private monopolistic actors enter the space, creating supply gaps and driving up costs, which contradicts the government's goal of 'affordable' service.

Paragraph 3: Analysis of Bottleneck

The stem notes that the transport service is interested in serving the people but fails due to a severe shortage of buses and trucks. This creates a supply-side bottleneck; without sufficient affordable transport, the industrial units in regional areas struggle with high logistics costs and supply chain delays, effectively weakening the foundation of the government's industrialization plan.

Paragraph 4: Evaluative Conclusion

In conclusion, the government must prioritize the modernization and expansion of its transport fleet to bridge the gap between its strategic goals and operational reality. By increasing the number of vehicles, the government can effectively lower the cost of doing business in rural areas, ensure smooth logistical support for new industries, and successfully realize its vision of decentralized national economic growth.

fullCQData.stem: To establish safe transportation for the country's people, the government passed a law in parliament to form an institution. The institution is incurring losses due to various mismanagement. To reduce losses, the government is thinking about other alternatives and trying to improve the quality of service.

questions.label: a
questions.text: PPP বলতে কী বোঝায়?
questions.answer: PPP stands for Public-Private Partnership, which is a collaborative arrangement between government agencies and private sector companies to fund, build, and operate public projects.

questions.label: b
questions.text: কীভাবে রাষ্ট্রীয় ব্যবসায় প্রাকৃতিক সম্পদের সর্বোত্তম ব্যবহার করে? ব্যাখ্যা করো।
questions.answer:
State-owned businesses ensure the best use of natural resources by preventing private exploitation and ensuring national interests are prioritized. By maintaining direct control over resources like forests, minerals, and water, the state prevents waste, ensures sustainable extraction, and uses the generated revenue for public welfare and national infrastructure development.

Furthermore, state-owned enterprises manage resources through long-term planning rather than short-term profit motives. This allows for scientific management of resources, ensuring that industrial development does not exhaust national assets prematurely and that the benefits of resource utilization are distributed equitably across the entire population.

questions.label: c
questions.text: উদ্দীপকে কোন প্রতিষ্ঠানের কথা বলা হয়েছে? ব্যাখ্যা করো।
questions.answer:
The institution mentioned in the stem is a Statutory Corporation (রাষ্ট্রীয় বিধিবদ্ধ সংস্থা). A statutory corporation is an autonomous entity created by a specific law passed in the national parliament to carry out specialized public services, such as transportation or utility supply.

These institutions are characterized by their own legal personality and a separate administrative structure. The government provides the initial capital, and the institution is run by a board of directors appointed by the government, operating independently while remaining accountable to the legislature.

Since the stem mentions that the government passed a law in parliament specifically to establish this transportation institution, it fits the definition and legal framework of a statutory corporation perfectly.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "দক্ষ ব্যবস্থাপনার অভাবই প্রতিষ্ঠানটির লোকসানের অন্যতম কারণ" -যুক্তি দেখাও।
questions.answer:
The statement "Lack of efficient management is a primary cause of the institution's losses" is highly justified in the context of state-owned enterprises.

Firstly, state-owned institutions often suffer from bureaucratic red tape and the absence of a competitive environment. Without the pressure to maximize profit like private firms, management often becomes complacent, leading to operational inefficiencies, overstaffing, and high administrative overheads.

Secondly, the lack of professional accountability often results in poor decision-making. When management is appointed based on political influence rather than professional merit, strategic planning suffers, leading to poor service delivery which drives away customers and reduces revenue, ultimately resulting in recurring losses.

Finally, because these organizations do not always face the threat of bankruptcy, there is often little incentive for the administration to adopt modern technology or cost-saving measures. Therefore, without a shift towards professional, results-oriented management and modernized systems, such an institution will continue to struggle financially despite being created for public good.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Ahmed Ali is a government employee. When the prices of essential commodities increase significantly in the market, Mr. Ahmed Ali's organization arranges for the sale of goods at lower prices. This benefits the public. This institution prioritizes public welfare without considering profit.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is business?
questions.answer: Business is any legitimate economic activity related to the production, distribution, and exchange of goods and services with the aim of earning profit.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by a buying house?
questions.answer:
A buying house is an intermediary organization that typically acts as a bridge between buyers and sellers, often in the garment or textile industry. They are primarily responsible for collecting and supplying products from local factories to foreign buyers according to their specifications, quality, and price requirements.

They also handle quality control, ensure timely shipment, and conduct inspections on behalf of the buyer. In simple terms, although a buying house does not produce goods directly, it plays a vital role in business operations by facilitating the buying and selling process.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What is the name of Mr. Ahmed Ali's institution? Explain.
questions.answer:
The institution mentioned in the stem is a State-Owned Enterprise or a state-welfare organization. State-owned business refers to an entity owned and operated by the government, where the primary objective is not to earn profit, but to ensure public welfare.

These types of institutions are established by law and governed by government policy. Since the institution in the stem is prioritizing public welfare by delivering essential goods at lower prices without considering profit, it is a clear example of a state-welfare business.

In conclusion, when the government ensures the supply of products to the market during times of public emergency and plays a role in controlling prices, such activities are essentially performed through a state-owned business structure. The institution in the stem is fulfilling that welfare-oriented purpose.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Should the activities of the institution described in the stem be expanded? Write with arguments.
questions.answer:
Yes, the activities of the institution described in the stem should definitely be expanded. The arguments in favor of this are discussed below:

1. Ensuring Public Welfare: The main objective of a state-welfare institution is to serve the people. When public life is disrupted by the abnormal price hike of essential goods, expanding the activities of this institution will allow the general public to purchase products at affordable prices, helping them maintain their standard of living.

2. Preventing Market Instability: Unscrupulous traders often create artificial crises to hike prices. If a state institution can supply products on a large scale, market instability decreases, and traders are unable to engage in excessive profiteering, which maintains balance in the entire market.

3. Economic Security: It is the government's responsibility to meet the basic needs of the country's people. If the activities of such institutions are expanded to every region of the country, marginalized people will also benefit, which fosters social stability and strengthens the foundation of the country's overall economic security.

4. Government Oversight and Accountability: Expanding operations leads to increased government supervision and improved service quality. If the scope of this institution can be increased through modern management and the use of technology, it will play a crucial role in maintaining market stability not only during disasters but also during normal times.

fullCQData.stem: Cox's Bazar is one of the tourist cities in Bangladesh. There are numerous public and private hotels and motels here. Although several five-star hotels have been established through private initiatives, government-operated hotels are more acceptable to people. Currently, options for traveling to Cox's Bazar outside of road transport are very limited. Considering the needs of general tourists, it is essential to extend the government-run public transport, which is currently operated exclusively, from Chittagong to Cox's Bazar. This will further enrich this tourist city of the country.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is an agreement/contract deed?
questions.answer: A written document created to determine the mutual rights and obligations among business partners is called a partnership agreement.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by copyright?
questions.answer: Copyright is a type of intellectual property right that grants a creator control over their artistic work, literature, music, or creative endeavors for a specific period. Through this, no one else can copy, distribute, or display the work without the creator's permission, which plays an important role in protecting intellectual property.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Under which organization are the government hotels and motels described in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
The government-run hotels and motels in Cox's Bazar mentioned in the stem are primarily under the "Bangladesh Parjatan Corporation." Bangladesh Parjatan Corporation is a statutory organization of the government that works for the development, promotion, and expansion of the tourism industry.

This organization plays a role in improving the quality of the tourism business by building infrastructure in the tourism sector and providing accommodation, food, and transportation facilities for tourists. The stem mentions that government hotels are more acceptable to people, which reflects the quality service and trust associated with this institution.

Therefore, the responsibility of ensuring government accommodation and other facilities in the tourist city of Cox's Bazar has been carried out by the Bangladesh Parjatan Corporation, which is directly part of the government's plans and policies.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the importance of the expansion of public transport to Cox's Bazar as mentioned in the stem.
questions.answer:
Yes, the proposal to expand government public transport to Cox's Bazar as mentioned in the stem is very timely, and its importance in expanding the tourism industry is immense. Its importance is evaluated below:

1. Expansion of the Tourism Industry: Cox's Bazar has huge potential as a tourist center. The expansion of public transport will make travel easier for tourists, thereby increasing the number of tourists and overall expanding the tourism industry.

2. Safe and Affordable Travel: In private transport, fares are often uncontrolled, and passengers have to face harassment. If there is government public transport, tourists will be able to travel at fixed fares and in a safe environment, which is very important for general tourists.

3. Economic Prosperity: An increase in tourists will boost hotels, restaurants, and local small businesses in Cox's Bazar. On one hand, this will create local employment, and on the other hand, it will increase the government's revenue, contributing significantly to the national economy.

4. Planned Infrastructure Development: There is no alternative to government transport to handle the pressure of road travel and maintain the balance of the tourist city. An expanded public transport system will help build a disciplined and modern transport system for tourists, which will be helpful in transforming Cox's Bazar into an international-standard tourist destination.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Ahmed Ali is a government employee. When the prices of essential commodities increase significantly in the market, Mr. Ahmed Ali's organization arranges for the sale of goods at lower prices. This benefits the public. This institution prioritizes public welfare without considering profit.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is business?
questions.answer: Business is any legitimate economic activity related to the production, distribution, and exchange of goods and services with the aim of earning profit.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by a buying house?
questions.answer:
A buying house is an intermediary organization that acts as a bridge between buyers and sellers, particularly in the apparel and textile sectors. They assist foreign buyers by sourcing high-quality products from local factories at competitive prices.

Furthermore, they manage quality control, ensure on-time shipments, and perform inspections on behalf of the buyer, playing a critical role in facilitating international trade without directly manufacturing the products themselves.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What is the name of Mr. Ahmed Ali's institution? Explain.
questions.answer:
The institution mentioned in the stem is a State-Owned Enterprise or a state-welfare organization, such as the Trading Corporation of Bangladesh (TCB). These organizations are government-owned and operated with the primary goal of public service rather than profit maximization.

These entities are established to stabilize market prices during crises, ensuring essential goods are available to the public at affordable rates. Their operations are guided by government policy and parliamentary mandates to protect consumer interests.

Since the institution is described as prioritizing public welfare over profit by selling essential goods at lower prices during market inflation, it functions as a critical mechanism for the state to maintain social and economic stability.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Should the activities of the institution described in the stem be expanded? Write with arguments.
questions.answer:
Yes, the activities of the institution described in the stem should be expanded. The arguments for this are as follows:

1. Controlling Market Prices: Expanding this institution’s reach helps stabilize market prices by increasing the supply of essential goods, preventing artificial inflation caused by syndicates and unscrupulous traders.

2. Ensuring Consumer Access: An expanded network ensures that low-income and marginalized populations, not just those in urban centers, have access to basic necessities at affordable prices, thereby directly reducing the cost of living.

3. Strengthening Economic Security: By providing a consistent supply of food and essential goods, the government can maintain national economic stability and social peace, which is vital during periods of economic hardship.

4. Improved Operational Efficiency: If the government scales up these operations, it can adopt better logistics, digital supply chain tracking, and modernized infrastructure, making the service more transparent, efficient, and accessible to a larger segment of the population.

fullCQData.stem: The Bangladesh government is implementing a project to launch a train service from Chittagong to Cox's Bazar through its own institution so that tourists can travel to Cox's Bazar safely and comfortably. On the other hand, the government has taken the initiative to form a new business to provide a Dhaka-Chittagong bullet train service with the assistance of private investors in addition to its own investment.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is WASA?
questions.answer: WASA stands for Water Supply and Sewerage Authority, which is a state-owned statutory organization responsible for providing water supply, sewerage, and drainage services in urban areas of Bangladesh.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why are state-owned businesses formed?
questions.answer: State-owned businesses are formed to ensure public welfare, maintain stability in the supply of essential goods and services, and manage sectors critical to national security or the economy that are not suitable for purely profit-driven private management. They aim to prevent monopolies, protect consumer interests, and ensure equitable distribution of resources.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which government organization is implementing the project for the safe and comfortable travel of tourists mentioned in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
The government organization implementing the train service project mentioned in the stem is the "Bangladesh Railway" (বাংলাদেশ রেলওয়ে). Bangladesh Railway is a state-owned, government-operated statutory organization responsible for the development, operation, and management of the country’s rail network.

As a key component of the state infrastructure, Bangladesh Railway operates under the Ministry of Railways to provide safe, reliable, and affordable transportation for the public. The project to connect Chittagong and Cox's Bazar is a strategic development aimed at boosting tourism and improving regional connectivity.

Therefore, since the train service is a state-led public transport initiative intended to benefit the general public and tourists, it is being executed by Bangladesh Railway as the authorized governmental entity.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Explain the significance of the new business mentioned in the stem in the context of constructing a large international airport on the other side of the Padma Bridge.
questions.answer:
The new business mentioned in the stem refers to a Public-Private Partnership (PPP). The initiative to construct a large international airport on the other side of the Padma Bridge would hold significant value if supported by such a model for the following reasons:

1. Capital Mobilization: Constructing a large-scale international airport requires massive financial investment. A PPP model allows the government to combine its resources with private sector funds, reducing the burden on the national exchequer and ensuring faster project completion.

2. Efficiency and Expertise: Private partners bring professional management, technological expertise, and operational efficiency to infrastructure projects. This ensures that the airport is built and operated to international standards, providing better facilities for passengers and cargo.

3. Integrated Connectivity: Just as the bullet train service is planned to connect major cities, the airport needs an efficient transport network. An integrated transport strategy—connecting the airport via high-speed rail or upgraded road networks using the PPP model—would significantly increase the viability and accessibility of the airport.

4. Economic Growth and Development: Such a collaborative model fosters a business-friendly environment, attracts foreign direct investment (FDI), and accelerates regional development. It would turn the area around the Padma Bridge into a massive economic hub, enhancing trade, tourism, and overall economic growth for the country.

fullCQData.stem: Mrs. Hasnat is a professor at the University of Dhaka. She lives in Dhaka with her family. She has decided to go to her village home for the upcoming Eid. However, she is worried about transportation. Although there are private buses, there are two types of special organizations for the safe and affordable travel of the people of Bangladesh. There are some complaints about the quality of service. The two organizations are not known as profitable institutions. The government is thinking of reducing the losses of the two organizations and improving the quality of service. Additionally, it has taken the initiative to launch new routes. For this, the government has arranged for the necessary funds.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is WASA?
questions.answer: WASA stands for Water Supply and Sewerage Authority, which is a state-owned statutory organization responsible for providing water supply, sewerage, and drainage services in urban areas of Bangladesh.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by a public welfare institution?
questions.answer: Public welfare institutions (জনকল্যাণমূলক প্রতিষ্ঠান) are state-owned or government-supported entities whose primary objective is to provide essential services to the public at affordable prices rather than maximizing profits. These institutions focus on social wellbeing, equitable resource distribution, and maintaining stability in the supply of essential goods and services for the benefit of the general public.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What are the two special organizations described in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
The two special organizations described in the stem are the Bangladesh Road Transport Corporation (BRTC) and the Bangladesh Railway. Both are state-owned enterprises focused on providing safe, reliable, and affordable public transportation across the country.

BRTC operates state-owned buses, and Bangladesh Railway operates the rail network. Both organizations are mandated by the government to serve the public, particularly during holidays or times of high demand, to ensure that citizens have access to affordable travel options when private sector providers might otherwise inflate prices.

Although these organizations often face challenges such as operational inefficiencies or recurring financial losses, they are essential for national connectivity and play a critical role in supporting low- and middle-income travelers who rely on these government-run services.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Evaluate the role of the two organizations in the stem in preventing monopoly business.
questions.answer:
The two organizations (BRTC and Bangladesh Railway) play a crucial role in preventing monopoly business in the transportation sector in the following ways:

1. Price Stabilization: Private transport owners often form syndicates during peak times like Eid to charge exorbitant fares. State-owned organizations provide affordable, fixed-price alternatives, which prevent private operators from maintaining a monopoly on pricing.

2. Ensuring Service Availability: By operating on various routes, these organizations ensure that there is a consistent supply of transportation services, preventing private operators from creating artificial shortages to manipulate the market.

3. Maintaining Competitive Pressure: The presence of a government-run, nationwide transport network creates a competitive environment that discourages private bus companies from exploiting passengers, as travelers have the choice to opt for government services if private fares become too high.

4. Social Responsibility: As these organizations prioritize welfare over profit, they serve routes that might be considered "unprofitable" by private firms, ensuring that all regions have access to transport and preventing any single private player from controlling or neglecting specific regions of the country.

fullCQData.stem: Due to a lack of technology and technical expertise, a country's government constructed a subway in one of its cities jointly with a foreign construction company. The country's government provided the capital. The foreign company will be responsible for its management. After 10 years, the country's government will take over the management responsibilities.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is State-Owned Business?
questions.answer: State-Owned Business refers to business enterprises that are owned, managed, and controlled by the government, with the primary objective of public service and welfare rather than profit maximization.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by PPP (Public-Private Partnership)?
questions.answer: PPP (Public-Private Partnership) is a collaborative arrangement between government agencies and private sector companies to fund, build, operate, and maintain public projects, combining public oversight with private sector efficiency and expertise.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of PPP is described in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
The PPP model described in the stem is the "Build-Operate-Transfer" (BOT) model.

In a BOT contract, the private party (in this case, the foreign company) is responsible for the design, construction, and operation of a project for a specified period (10 years here). During this time, they manage the facility and often derive revenue from it to recover their investment and operational costs. After the expiration of the concession period, the ownership and management responsibility are transferred back to the public authority (the government).

This model is particularly useful for governments that lack the necessary technical expertise or technology but have the capital, allowing them to benefit from private sector efficiency for a duration before assuming full control.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "Your win, my win" - it is a relationship of this kind for both the government and the foreign company - do you agree? Explain in detail.
questions.answer:
Yes, I agree that the partnership described in the stem represents a "win-win" relationship for both the government and the foreign company.

Government's Perspective (Winning):

1. Access to Expertise: The government gains access to advanced technology and technical expertise, which they lacked, enabling the successful completion of a complex project like a subway.

2. Long-term Asset: After 10 years, the government will inherit a fully functional, operational subway system without having had to manage the complexities of the initial construction and early operation phase.

3. Public Welfare: The project directly serves the citizens, addressing infrastructure needs that the government could not have met efficiently on its own.

Foreign Company's Perspective (Winning):

1. Business Opportunity: The company secures a significant construction contract and a long-term management role, providing them with a steady stream of income over the 10-year operational period.

2. Market Presence: Engaging in such a high-profile international project enhances the company's reputation, expertise, and global portfolio, opening doors for future contracts in the region.

3. Recovering Investment: While the government provided the capital, the company manages the operations, ensuring that the management process is optimized to deliver returns on the contract value over the designated 10 years.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Zahid joined an organization after completing his Master's degree. The organization will collect tolls for a specified period and later come under government control. On the other hand, Mr. Zahid's friend Mr. Rafi is working in an organization that provides training to the people with the aim of providing skilled human resources. The trained population from its training centers is creating new employment opportunities through tourism activities and establishing themselves as entrepreneurs. However, due to various limitations including frequent changes in government policies, surviving in competition is a major challenge for such businesses.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is statutory duty?
questions.answer: Statutory duty refers to the obligations mandated by law that an organization or individual must fulfill in accordance with its constitution or government regulations.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Who purchases qualification shares and why? Explain.
questions.answer:
Qualification shares are the minimum number of shares required to become a director of a company.

According to company law, a director must own a specific number of shares to be elected or to hold the position. This requirement is mandatory to ensure directors are loyal to the company and shareholders' interests, and to ensure accountability regarding business risks.

questions.label: c
questions.text: In what type of organization is Mr. Zahid working? Describe.
questions.answer:
The organization where Mr. Zahid is working is a project-based firm under the "Build-Operate-Transfer" (BOT) model.

In such organizations, the government enters into a contract with a private party where the private entity builds infrastructure (like a bridge or road) and collects tolls for a specified period to recover costs. The stem mentions that the organization will collect tolls for a set time and then come under government control, which directly indicates the characteristics of a BOT model.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Identify the type of organization Mr. Rafi works in and analyze the limitations that make surviving in competition a challenge for such businesses.
questions.answer:
Mr. Rafi works in a "State Welfare Organization," which focuses on public welfare and human resource development.

The limitations and challenges for such businesses to survive in competition are analyzed below:

1. Frequent Changes in Government Policy: The activities of state organizations depend on government policy decisions. Frequent changes hinder the implementation of long-term plans, making it difficult to compete in the market.

2. Bureaucratic Complexity: State organizations often suffer from slow decision-making and bureaucratic hurdles. Failure to keep pace with the rapid changes in the private sector causes them to fall behind in competition.

3. Lack of Efficiency and Accountability: These organizations often lack professional management and suffer from accountability issues. The innovative and efficient management required to compete with private entrepreneurs is often absent.

4. Political Influence: Political influence often affects the management of these organizations, which ruins a neutral business environment and reduces institutional efficiency. Overcoming these limitations is essential for achieving their goals.

fullCQData.stem: The Java Company Limited is a manufacturing organization of which 51% of shares are owned by the government. The organization helps to keep the food supply for the country's people regular. Due to a capital crisis, the company is failing to provide suitable services and is thinking of collecting capital by selling shares to the public. This will make it possible to provide suitable services and expand the business at the same time. The persons in responsible positions are asked to take necessary measures in this regard.
fullCQData.meta: Jalalabad Cantonment Public School and College, Sylhet · 2026

questions.label: a
questions.text: Write the full form of BTTB.
questions.answer: The full form of BTTB is Bangladesh Telegraph and Telephone Board.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Explain the importance of state-owned businesses in creating employment.
questions.answer:
State-owned businesses play a crucial role in creating employment. The establishment of state-owned industrial factories and service-oriented institutions creates employment opportunities for a large number of the country's unemployed population, which contributes to the country's overall economic development.

Additionally, the government directly plays a role in poverty alleviation and improving the standard of living by employing skilled, semi-skilled, and unskilled labor in various industrial and service sectors under state business operations. Such institutions help increase the purchasing power of the people and eliminate unemployment through employment.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of business is described in the stem based on ownership? Explain.
questions.answer:
The business described in the stem is a "Government Company" or a state-owned company based on ownership.

If at least 51% of the total paid-up capital of any company is owned by the government, in the name of the President, or by any other institution or organization on behalf of the government, it is called a government company. It is clearly stated in the stem that the government owns 51% of the shares of The Java Company Limited, which places it under the definition of a government company.

Although this type of institution is operated as a separate legal entity, the control and the major portion of ownership remain in the hands of the government. Therefore, the institution in the stem is a government company.

questions.label: d
questions.text: If capital is collected through the sale of shares, do you think there will be any change in the ownership of The Java Company Ltd.? Write with reasons.
questions.answer:
Yes, I think there will be a significant change in the ownership structure of The Java Company Limited if capital is collected through the sale of shares. The reasons are presented below:

1. Decentralization of Ownership: Currently, the government owns 51% of the company's shares. If new shares are issued to the public and the government's share rate falls below 51% due to public participation, it will no longer be considered a government company but will turn into a mixed-ownership or private company.

2. Change in Management and Control: With the change in ownership rate, the government's absolute control over the company's board of directors and policy-making power will decrease. As the participation of private or general shareholders increases, their influence in the decision-making process will increase.

3. Accountability and Transparency: If new shareholders join, the pressure for accountability and transparency in the company will increase. The institution will be more focused on profit-making and efficient service, as individual shareholders will expect good profits against their investments.

4. Change in the Nature of Ownership: Even if the government's partnership does not fall below 51% through the sale of shares, the density of ownership will decrease, and the company will be operated in a more democratic structure. In short, this change in ownership will make the institution more competitive and dynamic.

fullCQData.stem: The newly independent state named X entered into contracts with some private organizations for the development of its communication system. According to the contract terms, those organizations constructed various bridges, flyovers, and metro rails. The government will receive the earned profit alongside those organizations. Recently, the country has decided to establish an arms manufacturing factory to strengthen its security system.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is state-owned business?
questions.answer: State-owned business refers to business enterprises owned and operated by the government, where the primary objective is to ensure public welfare and maintain control over critical economic sectors rather than maximizing profit.

questions.label: b
questions.text: How does state-owned business prevent monopolistic trends?
questions.answer:
State-owned business prevents monopolistic trends in the following ways:

1. Reducing Artificial Scarcity: When private traders create artificial shortages of goods to raise prices, state-owned businesses ensure adequate supply in the market, thereby neutralizing that effect.

2. Providing Goods and Services at Affordable Prices: Government institutions supply goods and services at lower prices for public welfare, which prevents private institutions from achieving excessive profits.

3. Regulatory Role: Since the government maintains exclusive control over natural resources or critical service sectors, no specific private entity can control the entire market.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which type of business structure is similar to the first phase of work in state X? Explain.
questions.answer:
The first phase of work in state X, involving contracts for the construction of bridges, flyovers, and metro rails, is similar to the "PPP" (Public-Private Partnership) business structure.

PPP is a contractual arrangement where the government and the private sector jointly invest and work on infrastructure development or service projects. Since the government and private organizations are working together on the condition of sharing profits, this is a clear example of the PPP model.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Which business organizational structure do you think is logical to choose for implementing the decision taken in the stem? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
In my opinion, it is most logical to choose a "State-Owned Business" structure for implementing the decision to establish an arms manufacturing factory. The reasons in favor of this are:

1. National Security: An arms factory is an extremely sensitive institution involving national security. If it were in private hands, there could be security risks; it is possible to maintain strict control if it is operated by the government.

2. Maintaining Confidentiality: In the case of defense equipment production, it is essential to protect technical data and secrecy, which can only be ensured in a state-controlled institution.

3. Protecting Public Interest: The production or supply of items like weapons or defense equipment should not be aimed at private profit. If it is operated as a state-owned business, the government has full control over its use and distribution, which is vital for protecting the country's sovereignty.

fullCQData.stem: Private entrepreneurs like to do business in well-organized business centers. But the government wants to establish industries and businesses everywhere. In this case, the government does not calculate profit and loss. The Bangladesh government wants to utilize these opportunities everywhere through BRTC. This institution should be continued considering the convenience of the public even if it does not make a profit.
fullCQData.meta: Bangladesh Air Force Shaheen College, Chittagong · 2026

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a Right Share?
questions.answer: A right share is a preferential right of a company's existing shareholders to buy new shares in proportion to their held shares.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Can a minor be a partner? Explain.
questions.answer:
According to the partnership law, a minor or an individual under 18 years of age cannot generally become a partner because contractual capacity requires the person to be a major and of sound mind.

However, with the consent of all other partners, a minor can be added as a beneficiary of the partnership business solely to share profits. In this case, the minor cannot be a general or active partner of the institution and is not personally liable for any debts or liabilities of the business.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Through the government's desire to build industries, which objective of state-owned business is reflected? Explain.
questions.answer:
Through the government's desire to build industries, the objective of "Balanced Regional Development" (সুষম আঞ্চলিক উন্নয়ন) of state-owned business is reflected.

Entrepreneurs usually want to do business in developed infrastructure and urban-centric areas for the sake of profit. But the government wants to create employment and eliminate economic disparity by expanding industries and businesses throughout the country's remote areas.

To achieve balanced regional development, the government takes the initiative to establish business institutions in underdeveloped or remote areas, so that local people get facilities and the overall economic growth of the whole country is ensured. The government's desire to establish industries and businesses everywhere, as mentioned in the stem, primarily reflects this goal.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Explain the justification for the public transport mentioned in the stem.
questions.answer:
The justification for continuing a public transport system like BRTC, as mentioned in the stem, is immense. Its justifications are explained below:

1. Public Welfare and Social Responsibility: The main objective of state-owned business is not profit, but public welfare. Even if BRTC is not profitable, it is essential to ensure the travel of general people.

2. Maintaining Market Equilibrium: Privately owned transport syndicates often charge excessive fares to passengers. If there is a state transport system, it exerts pressure on them, resulting in controlled fares and affordable services for the general public.

3. Travel Facilities in Remote Areas: Private entrepreneurs usually do not want to run buses on remote or unprofitable routes. BRTC provides services on these routes in the public interest, which ensures the right of all people in the country to travel.

4. Services in Emergencies: During disasters or special needs, BRTC is capable of providing rapid services under government orders, which is not always possible for the private sector. So, even if BRTC is not profitable, it is essential in the interest of the national economy.

fullCQData.stem: Sylhet Gas Field Limited is supplying gas to chemical fertilizer and power generating companies at low prices without prioritizing profit earning, which is playing an important role in the country's economic development. On the other hand, to further strengthen the economic foundation, the government is connecting private organizations alongside the government in infrastructure construction. As a result, both the government and private organizations are benefiting. This new business concept is currently becoming popular in various countries of the world.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is the full form of BPC?
questions.answer: The full form of BPC is Bangladesh Petroleum Corporation.

questions.label: b
questions.text: How does state-owned business control market price? Explain.
questions.answer:
State-owned business controls market price in various ways:

1. Ensuring adequate supply of goods: When an artificial shortage of any product is created in the market, state-owned institutions resolve it by supplying sufficient products, keeping prices normal.

2. Supplying goods at affordable prices: Public welfare-oriented state institutions prioritize people's purchasing power over profit, which counters the excessive profit-seeking tendency of private traders.

3. Direct price fixing: In the case of essential goods or services, the government itself sets the prices, which helps stabilize market prices.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Under which type of business organization does Sylhet Gas Field Limited fall? Explain.
questions.answer:
Sylhet Gas Field Limited falls under the "State-Owned Business" category.

Institutions that are directly owned by the government and operated under government control and management are called state-owned businesses. The stem mentions that the institution is supplying gas for the sake of the country's economic development without prioritizing profit, which is a direct characteristic of public welfare-oriented state-owned business. It is an important service-oriented and strategic economic institution of the government.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the justification of the statement "The new business concept in the stem will strengthen the country's economic foundation."
questions.answer:
The new business concept mentioned in the stem is "Public-Private Partnership" (PPP), which is very effective in strengthening the country's economic foundation. Its validity is analyzed below:

1. Capital injection and risk distribution: Huge capital is required for massive infrastructure projects. In the PPP model, since both the government and the private sector invest, the lack of capital is removed, and risks are shared.

2. Increasing efficiency and innovation: The combination of the private sector's professionalism, modern technology, and efficiency increases the quality and speed of government work. This improves the quality of service and accelerates long-term economic growth.

3. Balanced development: Through the PPP model, the government can undertake more development projects even with limited resources, which helps in the development of communication systems and infrastructure in remote areas.

4. Benefit for both parties: The government can ensure development and public service, while private institutions get the opportunity to earn profit. As a result of this mutual cooperation, the overall economy becomes dynamic and the country's economic foundation is strengthened.

fullCQData.stem: Amir Hamza is a government employee. When the prices of essential commodities increase significantly in the market, Mr. Amir Hamza's institution arranges to sell products at lower prices. This benefits the people. This institution prioritizes public welfare over profit.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is business?
questions.answer: Business refers to any legal economic activity related to the production, distribution, and exchange of goods and services with the objective of earning profit.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by buying house?
questions.answer: A buying house is an intermediary institution that performs the duties of purchasing goods, ensuring quality control, and shipping products on time on behalf of a specific buyer (usually foreign). It acts as a bridge between the manufacturer and the buyer.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What is the type of Mr. Amir Hamza's institution? Explain.
questions.answer:
Mr. Amir Hamza's institution is a "State-Owned Business."

Business institutions that are directly owned and operated by the government are called state-owned businesses. The stem shows that the institution is managed by a government official and its primary goal is not to earn profit but to ensure public welfare by controlling the prices of essential commodities. Since it operates in the public interest under government control, it is definitely a state-owned business.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Should the activities of the institution mentioned in the stem be expanded? Describe with reasons.
questions.answer:
Yes, the activities of the institution described in the stem should definitely be expanded. The justifications are highlighted below:

1. Controlling Inflation and Stability: When the prices of essential goods rise abnormally, such institutions increase supply in the market to keep prices under control. Expanding activities will make market stability more lasting.

2. Protecting Low-Income People: A large portion of the country's population has limited purchasing power. If a government institution can reach them with products at affordable prices, it will play a significant role in protecting the living standards of the poor and general public.

3. Reducing Market Monopoly: Sometimes dishonest private traders create artificial shortages through syndicates. The presence and extensive activities of government institutions can frustrate these evil attempts.

4. Social Responsibility and Public Interest: The core philosophy of state-owned business is public welfare. Therefore, for the greater interest of the public and for the sake of economic security, the expansion of the scope and activities of such service-oriented institutions is extremely urgent.

fullCQData.stem: Due to the natural beauty of Sonadia Island, the government took an initiative to establish a hotel there. For internet facilities, seat reservations, and promoting other information, they are working jointly with another government institution. Local residents are employed to manage the institution. As they are providing modern facilities while conserving the forests and biodiversity, travelers' interest is increasing day by day.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is the reserve fund of a cooperative?
questions.answer: A reserve fund in a cooperative society is a portion of the annual net profit that is mandatorily set aside and accumulated to ensure the future financial stability, growth, and security of the society, as per legal requirements.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Explain the role of cooperative societies in reducing the influence of middlemen.
questions.answer: Cooperative societies reduce the influence of middlemen by establishing direct links between producers and consumers. They buy directly from producers and sell to members, eliminating layers of brokers who inflate prices, thus ensuring fair prices for both producers and consumers.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What is the name of the institution assisting in promoting information in the stem? Evaluate their activities.
questions.answer:
The institution assisting with information and reservations is likely a state-owned telecommunications or tourism authority.

Evaluation: Their activities are vital for modern business operations. By providing digital infrastructure (internet, reservations), they bridge the gap between remote natural attractions and urban tourists, promoting accessibility and efficiency in government tourism initiatives.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Do you think the hotel established in the first phase belongs to a type of business that is capable of boosting the country's economy? Analyze your opinion.
questions.answer:
Yes, the hotel established in the first phase, as a state-led infrastructure project, is highly capable of boosting the economy.

Analysis:

1. Tourism Revenue: It attracts both domestic and foreign tourists, generating foreign exchange and local revenue.

2. Employment Generation: The policy of employing local residents directly reduces regional unemployment and increases the purchasing power of the local population.

3. Infrastructure & Biodiversity: By balancing modern amenities with biodiversity conservation, it ensures sustainable tourism, which preserves the asset for long-term economic growth.

4. Multiplier Effect: The presence of a modern hotel encourages further development in transportation, local markets, and services, creating a positive multiplier effect on the overall national economy.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Nayeb Ali is a businessman. When the prices of commodities in the market suddenly rise, he sells essential goods in various parts of the country through TCB for the convenience of consumers. This benefits the people. This institution prioritizes public welfare over profit.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is state-owned business?
questions.answer: State-owned business refers to business enterprises owned and operated by the government, where the primary objective is to ensure public welfare and maintain control over critical economic sectors rather than maximizing profit.

questions.label: b
questions.text: "Public welfare is the main objective of state-owned business" - Explain.
questions.answer: The main goal of state-owned business is not to earn profit, but to achieve public welfare. The purpose of establishing state-owned business institutions is to ensure the availability of essential goods for the general public, keep product prices at a bearable level, and create employment by eliminating unemployment. Since these institutions are operated for the sake of fulfilling social responsibilities and overall economic development of the country rather than personal profit, public welfare is considered its main objective.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of business institution is TCB in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
The TCB (Trading Corporation of Bangladesh) mentioned in the stem is a "State-Owned Business" institution.

State-owned businesses are generally owned by the government and operate under government control and management. TCB is a government institution operated under the Ministry of Commerce of the Government of Bangladesh. The stem clearly states that it works for public welfare without thinking about profit and sells essential commodities, which is a direct characteristic of a state-owned service business.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the justification for expanding the activities of TCB described in the stem.
questions.answer:
The justification for expanding the activities of TCB described in the stem is very strong. The reasons are analyzed below:

1. Controlling Inflation: When the price of essential commodities rises abnormally in the market, increasing supply in the market through TCB reduces artificial scarcity and keeps product prices normal.

2. Protecting the General Public: Crises are created in the lives of low and middle-income people due to rising commodity prices. Expanding TCB's activities allows them to buy goods at affordable prices, which ensures their quality of life.

3. Breaking Syndicates: Many dishonest traders try to make excessive profits by creating syndicates and increasing prices. If TCB's activities are extended, syndicates become ineffective.

4. Economic Security: As part of the core philosophy of state-owned business, increasing the scope and service area of institutions like TCB is urgent for the interest of the national economy to ensure the economic security of the people.

fullCQData.stem: Mita went to visit her uncle's house in Natore and saw a large amount of sugarcane growing in this area. There is a large mill next to her uncle's house. The mill is a seasonal industry and the industry is a government institution. Her uncle said it is operated under an organization. Her uncle also said that there is a very large industrial organization in Bangladesh. Although many industries under it were privatized later, the fertilizer factory is still being operated under it.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Write the full form of BOT.
questions.answer: The full form of BOT is Build-Operate-Transfer.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by the Bangladesh Road Transport Corporation?
questions.answer: The Bangladesh Road Transport Corporation (BRTC) is a state-owned enterprise established by the government to provide reliable, safe, and affordable public transportation services across Bangladesh, helping to ensure mobility, particularly during peak times like holidays, and maintaining market competition against private transport operators.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Under which organization is the mill next to Mita's uncle's house operated? Explain.
questions.answer:
The mill next to Mita's uncle's house is a sugar mill, which is operated under the "Bangladesh Sugar and Food Industries Corporation" (BSFIC).

BSFIC is a state-owned industrial organization responsible for the management and operation of sugar mills in Bangladesh. Since the stem mentions a large mill producing sugar from sugarcane in Natore, which is a government institution, it directly refers to the sugar mills managed by BSFIC.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the role of the industrial organization mentioned later in the stem in the agricultural sector of the country.
questions.answer:
The industrial organization mentioned later in the stem is the "Bangladesh Chemical Industries Corporation" (BCIC).

Role in the agricultural sector:

1. Fertilizer Supply: BCIC operates several fertilizer factories that produce urea and other essential fertilizers. These are crucial for increasing agricultural productivity and ensuring food security in Bangladesh.

2. Affordable Pricing: By producing fertilizer domestically, the government can control prices and make it accessible to farmers at affordable rates, reducing dependency on expensive imports.

3. Technical Support and Stability: The continuous operation of state-run fertilizer factories provides stability to the agricultural supply chain, preventing the shortages that could severely hamper seasonal farming.

4. Economic Impact: By supporting the agricultural sector through the supply of essential inputs, BCIC contributes to the overall growth of the rural economy, impacting the livelihoods of millions of farmers across the country.

fullCQData.stem: Cox's Bazar is one of the tourism cities of Bangladesh. There are numerous public and private hotels and motels here. Although several five-star hotels have been established here through private initiatives, hotels managed under government agencies have much higher acceptability among people. Currently, the scope of going to Cox's Bazar is very limited outside of the road. Considering the general tourists, it is urgent to extend the only public transport facility currently managed under government ownership in Bangladesh, which started from the British period, from Chittagong to Cox's Bazar. This will further enrich this tourism city in the future.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is WASA?
questions.answer: WASA stands for Water Supply and Sewerage Authority. It is a government-owned service-oriented organization responsible for supplying water, sewerage, and drainage services in major cities of Bangladesh.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by PPP?
questions.answer: PPP (Public-Private Partnership) is a collaborative agreement between a government agency and a private sector company. It is used to finance, build, and operate projects such as public infrastructure (roads, bridges, airports) where the private sector brings efficiency and the government provides oversight and public interest protection.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Under which organization are the government-managed hotels and motels mentioned in the stem operated? Explain.
questions.answer:
The government-managed hotels and motels mentioned in the stem are operated under the "Bangladesh Parjatan Corporation" (BPC).

BPC is the national tourism organization responsible for developing tourism infrastructure and managing several government-owned tourism assets (such as hotels, motels, and restaurants) across Bangladesh.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the necessity of extending the public transport mentioned in the stem up to Cox's Bazar in the light of the stem.
questions.answer:
The public transport mentioned in the stem refers to the railway (Bangladesh Railway). Extending the railway from Chittagong to Cox's Bazar is essential for the following reasons:

1. Affordable Connectivity: The stem mentions that current travel options (mainly road) are limited and potentially expensive. A rail extension would provide a safe, affordable, and comfortable mode of transport for general tourists.

2. Economic Prosperity of the Tourism Sector: By making Cox's Bazar more accessible, the volume of tourists will increase significantly, leading to higher revenue for local businesses, hotels, and the tourism industry at large.

3. Reducing Pressure on Road Infrastructure: Relying solely on roads causes traffic congestion and increases the risk of accidents. Rail transport offers a more sustainable and high-capacity alternative for mass transit.

4. Socio-Economic Development: Improved accessibility will connect Cox's Bazar better with the rest of the country, promoting regional development, employment opportunities, and long-term socio-economic enrichment for the city.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Safwan went to Khulna with his family during the Eid holidays. He collected tickets via mobile SMS and reached Khulna by car from Dhaka Airport. They were very happy despite traveling a long distance because there was food arrangement inside. From Khulna, they used roads and waterways to visit the Sundarbans. Mr. Safwan thought that if adequate residential facilities and improved transportation could be ensured around the Sundarbans, it would be more attractive to domestic and foreign visitors. At the same time, it could enrich the country's economy.
fullCQData.meta: Abdul Kadir Molla City College, Narsingdi · 2026

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a qualification share?
questions.answer: A qualification share is the minimum number of shares a person must own to be eligible to become a director of a company, as mandated by the company's articles of association.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is the Certificate of Incorporation called the birth certificate of a company? Explain.
questions.answer: The Certificate of Incorporation is called the birth certificate because it is the legal document issued by the Registrar of Joint Stock Companies that brings the company into existence as a separate legal entity, marking the official date of its "birth" and commencement of legal life.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Explain the significance of the travel path used by Mr. Safwan from Dhaka to Khulna in the stem.
questions.answer:
The travel path used by Mr. Safwan involves using modern transportation infrastructure: an airport shuttle/transport service and established road/water networks.

Significance:

1. Efficiency: SMS-based ticketing and integrated transport services demonstrate the modernization of the transport sector.

2. Comfort and Integration: The inclusion of amenities (food) within the transport reflects a shift towards service-oriented public/private transportation, which is crucial for tourism.

3. Connectivity: This integrated path highlights the potential for seamless travel between major cities and remote tourist attractions, which is a prerequisite for a flourishing tourism industry.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the effectiveness of the state-owned institution that can play a role in implementing Mr. Safwan's idea in the light of the stem.
questions.answer:
The state-owned institution that can play the most significant role in implementing Mr. Safwan's idea is the "Bangladesh Parjatan Corporation" (BPC).

Effectiveness Analysis:

1. Infrastructure Development: BPC can spearhead the construction of modern hotels, motels, and tourist lodges in the Sundarbans area, addressing the residential facility gap.

2. Coordination: It can coordinate with other state-owned entities to improve transportation access to the Sundarbans.

3. Policy and Promotion: BPC can formulate policies to promote eco-friendly tourism that protects biodiversity while providing necessary amenities to tourists, directly aligning with Safwan's vision.

4. Economic Impact: By providing these facilities, BPC acts as a catalyst for economic growth, generating local employment, increasing foreign exchange earnings through international tourism, and boosting the overall tourism sector of the country.

fullCQData.stem: "X" company has a capital of 100 crore taka, out of which 52 crore taka is from the government and the remaining capital is collected through private funding. The institution has earned 20 crore taka in the current year.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is state-owned business?
questions.answer: State-owned business refers to business enterprises owned and operated by the government, where the primary objective is to ensure public welfare and maintain control over critical economic sectors rather than maximizing profit.

questions.label: b
questions.text: How does state-owned business make the best use of natural resources? Explain.
questions.answer:
State-owned businesses ensure the best use of natural resources in the following ways:

1. Prevention of Exploitation: Private businesses often exploit natural resources rapidly for personal gain, ignoring long-term sustainability. The government ensures planned extraction.

2. Strategic Planning: The government uses natural resources based on national priority and long-term economic plans, ensuring benefits for the entire country rather than a few individuals.

3. Technology and Conservation: Government-led projects often integrate advanced technology and biodiversity conservation measures, ensuring resources are utilized efficiently without exhausting them for future generations.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of institution is mentioned in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
The institution mentioned in the stem is a "Government Company".

According to the company law, if at least 51% of a company's total paid-up capital is owned by the government, it is classified as a government company. In the stem, company "X" has a total capital of 100 crore taka, and 52 crore taka (52%) is government-owned. Since the government's stake is more than 51%, it fits the definition of a government company perfectly.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the profit retention process of the institution mentioned in the stem.
questions.answer:
The profit retention and distribution process of a Government Company are governed by the company's articles of association and the Companies Act. The analysis is as follows:

1. Dividend to Shareholders: Since private investors are involved (holding 48% of the capital), the profit is distributed as dividends according to the ratio of shareholding. Both the government and the private shareholders receive their portion of the 20 crore taka profit.

2. Reserve and Surplus: A significant portion of the profit is typically set aside as reserve funds for future expansion, modernization of machinery, or to cover unexpected losses, ensuring the company's long-term sustainability.

3. Reinvestment: As a government-linked entity, the company often reinvests a portion of its profits into infrastructure, research, or public-interest projects to align with government goals.

4. Government Revenue: The portion of dividends received by the government is deposited into the national treasury, contributing to the country's overall economic development.

fullCQData.stem: The government has currently entrusted an organization with the responsibility of preserving ancient monuments and historical places. Historical sites in Bangladesh are now being preserved as part of cultural infrastructure. Using advanced GIS mapping, 3D scanning, and digital archives to create documentation, this specialized agency is applying an ecosystem model for the use and maintenance of heritage clusters. Hostels, resorts, eco-friendly tourism modules, and profit-maximization technologies are playing a huge role as a source of government revenue.

questions.label: a
questions.text: Write the full form of WASA.
questions.answer: WASA stands for Water Supply and Sewerage Authority.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by PPP business? Explain.
questions.answer: PPP (Public-Private Partnership) is a collaborative agreement between a government agency and a private sector company. It is used to finance, build, and operate projects such as public infrastructure (roads, bridges, airports, heritage sites) where the private sector brings efficiency and the government provides oversight and public interest protection.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of business organization is the agency mentioned in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
The agency mentioned in the stem is a "State-Owned Business" or more specifically, a state-owned specialized statutory authority.

State-owned businesses are enterprises owned and operated by the government. Since the agency is tasked with managing heritage sites (a public asset), applying national-level technological models, and generating revenue for the government, it operates as an instrument of state policy rather than a private profit-seeking entity. It serves the dual purpose of national heritage conservation (welfare) and government revenue generation.

questions.label: d
questions.text: How justified is the government's delegation of responsibility to this agency? Give your opinion.
questions.answer:
The government's delegation of responsibility to this agency is highly justified for the following reasons:

1. Expert Management: Preserving ancient monuments and historical sites requires specialized knowledge, modern technology (GIS, 3D scanning), and professional management, which a specialized state agency is best equipped to provide.

2. Sustainable Revenue Generation: By integrating modern tourism modules (resorts, eco-tourism) into heritage management, the agency ensures that these sites are not just a financial burden but a source of government revenue.
