fullCQData.stem: Ripon is a grocery shopkeeper in Rasulpur. Many people from the village live in Dhaka and send money home every month. Their relatives have to travel to the city to withdraw this money. Moreover, they have to spend an entire day going to a bank in the city to pay utility bills like electricity and gas bills. Observing these hassles, Ripon took an internet connection on his mobile handset. Now, the villagers complete these tasks directly at his shop through his mobile phone.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a credit card?
questions.answer: A credit card is a plastic or digital payment card issued by a bank or financial institution that allows the cardholder to buy goods and services on credit or withdraw cash up to a pre-approved financial limit.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What do you mean by e-commerce? Explain.
questions.answer:
E-commerce (electronic commerce) refers to the buying, selling, marketing, and distribution of products, services, or information over electronic networks, primarily the internet. It allows businesses and consumers to complete commercial transactions digitally without physically meeting.

In modern commerce, e-commerce eliminates geographical barriers, lowers operational overheads for businesses, and allows customers to shop 24/7. It includes various commercial models such as Business-to-Consumer (B2C) and Business-to-Business (B2B), transforming traditional trading into a fast, digital marketplace.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which type of banking method is Ripon using? Explain.
questions.answer:
Ripon is using a modern digital financial framework known as Mobile Banking (or M-Banking / Mobile Financial Services - MFS). This banking system uses cellular networks, internet-enabled handsets, and specialized software applications to execute secure financial transactions remotely, bypassing traditional physical bank branches.

In modern financial ecosystems, mobile banking serves as a vital tool for financial inclusion, especially in rural and remote regions. By converting a standard mobile phone into a digital transaction portal, it allows users to manage cash flows, transfer money, and settle bills instantly without needing a physical brick-and-mortar bank infrastructure.

The stem highlights that Ripon connected his mobile handset to the internet to help villagers receive remittances from Dhaka and pay their utility bills (electricity, gas) directly from his shop. Because these secure transactions are processed instantly through a mobile network and handheld device, his operational model perfectly matches the core traits of mobile banking.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "The adopted banking system in the stem has brought comfort to people's lives"—Do you agree with this statement? Give arguments in favor of your answer.
questions.answer:
Yes, I fully agree with the statement that mobile and electronic banking systems have brought immense comfort, safety, and efficiency to people's daily lives, especially for rural communities, through several key dimensions:

Firstly, Elimination of Geographical Barriers and Travel Cost Hassles: Under traditional banking setups, rural residents had to travel long distances to the nearest city and spend an entire day in long queues just to pay bills or withdraw money. Mobile banking brings these financial services directly to the village crossroads, saving valuable working hours and reducing travel expenses.

Secondly, Safe and Fast Inflow of Domestic Remittances: For families relying on income from relatives working in major cities like Dhaka, speed and security are critical. Mobile financial services allow fast money transfers, ensuring that rural dependents can access vital living funds instantly through local agents like Ripon, eliminating the risks of carrying physical cash or dealing with informal middlemen.

Thirdly, Streamlined Utility Bill Management: Paying utility bills digitally through a mobile handset removes the risk of administrative delays, late fees, and service disconnections. This automated payment network simplifies household management, allowing rural consumers to maintain continuous utility access through a few clicks on a phone screen.

Fourthly, In conclusion, mobile banking serves as an essential tool for digital inclusion and lifestyle modernization: It transforms local grocery stores into micro-banking hubs, improves the rural standard of living, and reduces the urban-rural economic divide. This makes digital banking an indispensable asset for building a modern, efficient, and financially secure society.

fullCQData.stem: Begum Laila is a modern housewife. Recently, she has been doing her clothing shopping through her mobile phone. This has significantly reduced her expenses. Thanks to Facebook, she can learn about various designs and prices of different clothes from the comfort of her home and place orders immediately. Moreover, she now completes various financial transactions, including bills for purchased clothes and electricity bills, from home. Seeing her, neighbors and others are now following the same method.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is information technology?
questions.answer: Information technology (IT) refers to the use of computers, storage, networking, and other physical devices, infrastructure, and processes to create, process, store, secure, and exchange all forms of electronic data.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is mobile banking so popular currently? Explain.
questions.answer:
Mobile banking is highly popular currently because of its unparalleled convenience, 24/7 accessibility, speed, and cost-effectiveness. It eliminates the need to visit physical bank branches or wait in long queues, allowing users to conduct secure transactions—such as money transfers, utility bill payments, and mobile recharges—instantly from anywhere using a cellular handset.

Furthermore, in developing nations, it drives financial inclusion by providing formal banking access to unbanked rural and semi-urban populations, reducing transactional costs and transit hassles significantly.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What kind of business can the clothing purchase described in the stem be called? Explain.
questions.answer:
The clothing purchasing method adopted by Begum Laila is classified as E-commerce (Electronic Commerce), specifically falling under F-commerce (Facebook Commerce) and Social Commerce. This refers to the buying and selling of goods, services, or information over electronic networks and social media platforms using digital devices like smartphones and laptops.

In modern business dynamics, social media platforms act as virtual marketplaces where businesses showcase catalogs, interact with customers, and receive orders directly. Consumers bypass traditional physical retail outlets, eliminating geographic constraints and intermediaries, which significantly lowers purchasing costs and enhances transactional convenience.

The stem notes that Begum Laila browses varied clothing designs and pricing details on Facebook from her home and instantly places orders via her mobile handset. Since the entire commercial cycle of product discovery, selection, and transaction initiation is conducted digitally over an internet-enabled social network, her activity represents a classic application of e-commerce.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Provide arguments in favor of the supporting business service that played a positive role behind Begum Laila's success.
questions.answer:
The supporting business service that played a vital role in Begum Laila's successful adoption of this modern lifestyle is Electronic Banking and Digital Financial Services (E-Banking / Mobile Financial Services). This support service provides the underlying financial infrastructure that makes remote digital commerce fully operational.

Firstly, Enabling Secure and Instant Remote Payments: E-commerce cannot thrive on visual browsing alone; it requires a reliable framework to settle transactions. By using mobile financial applications, Begum Laila can pay for her clothes and settle utility bills directly from her home, making her shopping experience entirely cashless and hassle-free.

Secondly, Significant Reduction in Transactional Expenses and Time: Traditional retail shopping requires physical travel, resulting in transportation costs, fuel consumption, and hours wasted in transit or crowds. The digital payment support infrastructure eliminates these operational overheads, allowing consumers to maximize their household savings and reallocate personal time efficiently.

Thirdly, Driving the Demonstration Effect and Social Inclusion: A key characteristic of successful operational support services is their scalability through community adaptation. As the stem highlights, Laila's streamlined lifestyle inspired her neighbors and others to adopt the same digital systems, demonstrating how reliable electronic banking services can spark a chain reaction of digital modernization at the grassroots level.

Fourthly, In conclusion, digital banking support serves as an essential pillar of modern consumer welfare: It seamlessly bridges the gap between digital merchants and remote buyers. Without secure cellular payment channels, online commerce would remain restricted, proving that electronic financial services are indispensable for enhancing the quality, safety, and efficiency of contemporary daily life.

fullCQData.stem: <svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="0 0 700 250" width="100%" height="100%"> <defs> <style> .title-text { font-family: 'Courier New', Courier, monospace, sans-serif; font-size: 22px; font-weight: bold; fill: #000000; } .node-text { font-family: 'Arial', sans-serif; font-size: 24px; font-weight: bold; fill: #000000; text-anchor: middle; } .box-text { font-family: 'Segoe UI', Tahoma, Geneva, Verdana, sans-serif; font-size: 16px; fill: #000000; font-weight: 500; } .box-shape { fill: #ffffff; stroke: #000000; stroke-width: 1.5; } .connection-line { fill: none; stroke: #000000; stroke-width: 2; } .arrow-head { fill: #000000; } </style> <marker id="arrow" viewBox="0 0 10 10" refX="6" refY="5" markerWidth="6" markerHeight="6" orient="auto-start-reverse"> <path d="M 0 1.5 L 7 5 L 0 8.5 z" class="arrow-head"/> </marker> </defs> <rect width="100%" height="100%" fill="#ffffff" /> <text x="95" y="45" class="title-text">Look at the chart below and answer the questions—</text> <circle cx="95" cy="140" r="32" class="box-shape" /> <text x="95" y="148" class="node-text">?</text> <path d="M 127,140 L 165,140 L 165,82 L 210,82" class="connection-line" marker-end="url(#arrow)" /> <path d="M 127,140 L 210,140" class="connection-line" marker-end="url(#arrow)" /> <path d="M 127,140 L 165,140 L 165,198 L 210,198" class="connection-line" marker-end="url(#arrow)" /> <rect x="215" y="62" width="470" height="40" class="box-shape" /> <text x="225" y="87" class="box-text">No obligation to have an account to use the card</text> <rect x="215" y="120" width="470" height="40" class="box-shape" /> <text x="225" y="145" class="box-text">Opportunity to purchase goods on credit exists</text> <rect x="215" y="178" width="470" height="40" class="box-shape" /> <text x="225" y="203" class="box-text">Paying off the card dues is mandatory</text> </svg>

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is a credit card?
questions.answer: A credit card is a plastic or digital payment card issued by a bank or financial institution that allows the cardholder to purchase goods and services on credit or withdraw cash up to a pre-approved financial limit.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What do you mean by e-marketing? Explain.
questions.answer:
E-marketing (Electronic Marketing) refers to the strategic process of planning, promoting, pricing, and distributing products or services over electronic networks, primarily the internet and digital media. It involves utilizing channels such as websites, emails, social networks, and search engines to connect with potential buyers and interact with an existing client base.

In contemporary commerce, e-marketing offers businesses a highly cost-effective alternative to traditional print or broadcast advertisements. It allows brands to monitor real-time consumer data, personalize promotional messages according to specific consumer preferences, and operate 24/7 globally without geographic limitations, making it a cornerstone of modern business expansion.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which service feature of electronic banking is indicated by the [ ? ] mark in the visible chart? Explain.
questions.answer:
The [ ? ] mark in the given chart indicates a Credit Card, a widely used electronic banking service. The listed features directly point to the functional characteristics that distinguish credit cards from other transactional tools like debit cards.

In modern banking ecosystems, electronic plastic cards are categorized based on their underlying funding source and account requirements. A standard debit card requires the cardholder to maintain an active bank account containing sufficient cash deposits, as funds are deducted instantly upon transaction execution. Conversely, a credit card is built on a pre-approved credit line extended by the financial institution to the cardholder, independent of their primary current or savings account balance.

The table explicitly highlights three core characteristics:

1. There is no strict requirement to have a pre-existing deposit account to use the card.

2. The cardholder enjoys a built-in facility to purchase products on credit.

3. Repaying the borrowed money within a designated grace period is legally mandatory.

Because these parameters describe a formal credit system rather than a direct debit mechanism, the hidden service in the diagram is unquestionably a credit card.

questions.label: d
questions.text: How justified is it to call the card a 'type of personal loan' based on the information mentioned in the chart? Analyze.
questions.answer:
Defining a credit card as a "Type of Unsecured Personal Loan" is completely logical, accurate, and aligned with standard financial economics due to several structural characteristics:

Firstly, Bypassing Immediate Asset Requirements: Traditional credit lines or collateral-backed business loans require the borrower to pledge assets or clear a lengthy approval process for each transaction. A credit card gives the user instant access to an unsecured personal revolving credit line, enabling them to make emergency purchases or buy everyday items on credit up to an authorized limit without signing a fresh loan contract at the cash register.

Secondly, Absence of Mandatory Deposit Accounts: When using a debit card, a customer spends their own pre-deposited money. However, a credit card does not require a linked deposit account, meaning the bank acts as a short-term lender. Every transaction represents an explicit personal micro-loan where the bank pays the merchant on behalf of the customer, expecting reimbursement at a later date.

Thirdly, Binding Financial Repayment Obligations: Just like any formal banking loan, the money utilized through a credit card is not free equity. The cardholder is under a strict legal obligation to pay back the borrowed amount. If they fail to clear the outstanding balance within the monthly statement cycle, the bank imposes high interest penalties and processing fees, mirroring standard personal loan default terms.

Fourthly, In conclusion, a credit card functions as an automated personal credit facility: It offers immediate liquidity based entirely on the cardholder's personal creditworthiness and income profile. Therefore, analyzing the chart's data confirms that a credit card is legally and functionally an accessible form of personal revolving loan designed to enhance modern consumer purchasing power.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Sami imports modern high-tech household appliances from abroad and sells them directly to consumers. Instead of setting up a physical retail store, he opened a corporate office and launched a web page named Megina.Com on the internet. On this web page, he displays the brand names, price lists, and photographs of all his products. When a consumer selects and orders a product, it is delivered directly to their designated address. In addition to assessing customer demand, Mr. Sami utilizes modern technologies such as e-mail, Facebook, and Twitter to ensure hassle-free product delivery. Consequently, his customer base is expanding daily, and Mr. Sami is earning substantial profits.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is information technology?
questions.answer: Information technology (IT) refers to the use of computers, storage, networking, and other physical devices, infrastructure, and processes to create, process, store, secure, and exchange all forms of electronic data.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by e-banking? Explain.
questions.answer:
E-banking (Electronic Banking) refers to a modern banking service that enables customers to conduct financial transactions digitally over secure electronic networks, primarily the internet, without visiting a physical brick-and-mortar bank branch. It encompasses services such as online fund transfers, automated teller machines (ATMs), mobile banking apps, and electronic utility bill payments.

In contemporary commercial ecosystems, e-banking provides 24/7 financial access, enhances transactional transparency, and drastically reduces processing time. It serves as the core financial backbone for remote trade, allowing businesses and individuals to instantly settle invoices and process cashless payments globally.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What type of e-business is Mr. Sami's Megina.Com described in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Mr. Sami's web venture, Megina.Com, is classified as a Business-to-Consumer (B2C) E-commerce model. This specific type of electronic business involves commercial transactions where an enterprise hosts a digital platform to market and sell products or services directly to individual end consumers, entirely bypassing traditional middlemen.

In structural business dynamics, B2C e-commerce eliminates the need for extensive physical retail frameworks, multi-tier distributor networks, and retail shelf space. Instead, businesses utilize internet-enabled portals as virtual storefronts where product specifications, imagery, and pricing structures are transparently published, enabling customers to browse and place direct purchase orders from anywhere.

The stem highlights that Mr. Sami setup an office and launched Megina.Com on the internet to showcase household appliances, complete with brand names, pictures, and price lists. Since individual consumers browse this platform from their homes, place direct orders, and receive shipments straight to their personal addresses without any intermediate wholesalers or retailers, his operational architecture perfectly represents the B2C e-commerce framework.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "Mr. Sami is earning substantial profits primarily due to e-marketing"—Evaluate this statement in light of the stem.
questions.answer:
Yes, the statement is completely accurate and justified because the strategic application of E-marketing (Electronic Marketing) has enabled Mr. Sami to optimize his operating costs, maximize consumer outreach, and streamline fulfillment, resulting in high profitability through several key dimensions:

Firstly, Dramatically Lower Operational Overheads: Traditional appliance retailing requires prime commercial real estate, elaborate showroom setups, and a large retail staff, all of which incur high fixed costs. By substituting a physical storefront with the digital platform Megina.Com, Mr. Sami minimized his structural rent and inventory maintenance expenses, allowing him to convert a higher percentage of gross revenue directly into net corporate profits.

Secondly, Precision Consumer Targeting via Social Platforms: Utilizing integrated digital channels like Facebook, Twitter, and e-mail allows a modern marketer to engage directly with tech-savvy consumer demographics. Mr. Sami leverages these platforms not just for generic broadcasting, but to analyze customer demands and gather real-time feedback, ensuring his product portfolio remains highly aligned with actual market preferences.

Thirdly, Seamless, Frictionless Customer Experience: Modern consumers prioritize speed, transparency, and personal safety. By publishing clear brand descriptions, transparent price lists, and high-definition photographs on his web page, and offering direct doorstep delivery, Mr. Sami eliminated traditional shopping hassles, which naturally drove higher repeat purchase rates and an expanding customer base.

Fourthly, In conclusion, digital marketing acts as a primary catalyst for scaling modern micro-enterprises: It bridges the gap between international supply chains and localized households with maximum efficiency. Mr. Sami's financial success proves that replacing conventional brick-and-mortar limitations with real-time digital marketing tools is the most viable strategy for achieving sustainable high-volume profitability in the modern economy.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Sohel established a Thai glass shop in Jessore. Every month, he used to send money to his parents through a traditional bank. This process consumed a significant amount of his time and money. Currently, due to the expansion of the mobile network in his village, he can now send money to his family quickly and safely. His father feels secure and happy about receiving the money.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is online shopping?
questions.answer: Online shopping is a form of electronic commerce that allows consumers to directly buy goods or services from a seller over the internet using a web browser or a mobile app.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What type of e-business is B2C? Explain.
questions.answer:
B2C (Business-to-Consumer) is a type of e-business model that involves commercial transactions conducted directly between a business enterprise and individual end consumers over electronic networks. In this model, the business uses online storefronts, websites, or mobile applications to market, sell, and distribute its products or services directly to individuals, completely bypassing traditional middle tier channels like wholesalers or physical brokers.

In modern business dynamics, B2C e-business provides maximum convenience to buyers by offering 24/7 access, home delivery, and transparent product listings. It allows manufacturers or service providers to lower their operational overheads, manage consumer portfolios directly, and scale their sales velocity, making it the most prominent framework in digital retail trade.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Through which banking facility can Mr. Sohel send money to his village as described in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Mr. Sohel is utilizing a modern digital financial facility known as Mobile Banking (Mobile Financial Services - MFS) to send money to his village. This banking system uses telecommunication networks, cellular handsets, and digital application protocols to execute instant financial transfers remotely without requiring either party to visit a physical bank branch.

In financial economics, mobile banking acts as a primary tool for financial inclusion, especially for expanding services to rural and remote populations. By converting a standard cellular connection into a transaction gateway, it allows individuals to transfer cash, settle invoices, and manage remittances instantly, eliminating the geographic barriers and long transit delays associated with traditional brick-and-mortar financial houses.

The stem highlights that Mr. Sohel previously suffered from a loss of time and money when using conventional banking lines from Jessore. However, with the expansion of cellular infrastructure in his home village, he can now process rapid, hassle-free money transfers directly to his parents via a mobile network. Because this transaction is processed instantly over a cellular platform using mobile devices, it perfectly aligns with the operational framework of mobile banking.

questions.label: d
questions.text: "Mr. Sohel's father is satisfied due to technology-driven banking facilities"—Provide your opinion with arguments.
questions.answer:
Yes, I completely agree with the statement that technology-driven banking facilities have brought immense convenience, security, and satisfaction to Mr. Sohel’s father, transforming his daily life through several key dimensions:

Firstly, Elimination of Geographic and Travel Bottlenecks: Traditional rural banking systems require elderly dependents to travel long distances to town branches, stand in long queues, and waste entire working days just to withdraw personal remittances. Mobile banking brings these financial services straight to the village crossroads or doorstep, completely removing physical exhaustion and travel hazards for senior citizens.

Secondly, Instant Access to Liquid Funds and Higher Financial Security: Under older manual transaction systems, sending money through banks or postal networks took days, creating financial anxiety during household emergencies. Mobile financial services ensure real-time fund delivery, allowing Mr. Sohel's father to receive instant text alerts and access his money immediately, which gives him peace of mind and financial security.

Thirdly, Mitigation of Financial Theft and Loss Risks: Carrying physical cash over long distances from urban bank branches to rural homes leaves individuals vulnerable to local pickpockets, fraudulent middlemen, or loss. Tech-driven banking secures the entire transaction cycle with personalized PIN numbers and automated ledgers, ensuring the money goes directly from the sender's handset to the recipient's secure account without any outside interference.

Fourthly, In conclusion, digital financial technology serves as an essential tool for lifestyle modernization and rural empowerment: It replaces outdated, slow processes with fast, secure digital networks. Mr. Sohel's father's satisfaction highlights how technological innovations in banking improve quality of life, strengthen family support networks, and bridge the urban-rural economic divide seamlessly.

fullCQData.stem: Mrs. Nila works in a private organization. She has to manage everything for her family. Due to being overly busy with her office work, she currently pays electricity, gas, and water bills through her mobile phone. She purchases daily household necessities over the internet from a shopping mall named 'Bikikini'. The shopping mall's delivery workers bring the items to Mrs. Nila's house, and she pays the price using her mobile phone. Mrs. Nila no longer has to face much hassle to pay bills and prices.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is ICT?
questions.answer: ICT stands for Information and Communication Technology, which refers to the unified integration of telecommunications, computers, software, and storage systems that enable users to access, store, transmit, and manipulate digital information.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is a credit card better than a debit card? Explain.
questions.answer:
A credit card is often considered better than a debit card because it offers a built-in short-term credit line, allowing the cardholder to buy goods, services, or withdraw cash instantly up to a pre-approved limit without needing immediate funds in a deposit account. Conversely, a debit card requires an active bank account with a sufficient cash balance, as funds are deducted instantly upon transaction.

Furthermore, credit cards provide superior financial flexibility during personal emergencies, offer robust fraud protection liabilities, and allow consumers to build a positive credit history, making them a more dynamic financial tool in modern commerce.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Through which process does Mrs. Nila in the stem pay her bills? Explain.
questions.answer:
Mrs. Nila pays her utility bills and product costs through Mobile Banking (Mobile Financial Services - MFS), which is an integral component of the broader Electronic Banking (E-Banking) system. This process leverages cellular networks, internet connectivity, and mobile applications to conduct secure financial transactions remotely without requiring the user to visit a physical bank or utility payment center.

In modern business ecology, mobile financial platforms serve as a vital transactional tool that brings banking services directly to the user's fingertips. By linking a user's mobile wallet to financial clearinghouses, it enables instantaneous, cashless settlement of invoices, utility bills, and e-commerce purchases, thereby bypassing traditional queues and geographical boundaries.

The stem highlights that due to her demanding corporate schedule, Mrs. Nila settles her utility bills (electricity, gas, water) and pays for her household items from 'Bikikini' directly through her mobile phone. Since these financial balances are transferred instantly from her digital wallet to the respective service providers and merchants using a mobile network, her payment methodology perfectly embodies the process of mobile banking.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Do you think the electronic technology-based enterprise 'Bikikini' is playing a massive role in business expansion? Provide arguments in favor of your answer.
questions.answer:
Yes, I completely agree with the view that the electronic technology-based shopping portal 'Bikikini' is playing a transformative, massive role in expanding business operations and redefining the modern marketplace through several key dimensions:

Firstly, Elimination of Geographic and Structural Constraints: Traditional retail storefronts require massive capital investments in prime real estate, physical showroom maintenance, and localized branches to reach customers. An e-commerce enterprise like 'Bikikini' operates via virtual storefronts over the internet, allowing it to display an unlimited inventory of products to a nationwide consumer base simultaneously without the overhead costs of physical expansion.

Secondly, Optimizing Consumer Convenience and Driving Sales Volume: Modern urban lifestyles are constrained by intense work schedules and traffic congestion, which discourage physical shopping trips. By offering an online catalog where household goods can be ordered effortlessly and delivered directly to the doorstep, 'Bikikini' captures a premium segment of busy consumers like Mrs. Nila, increasing purchase frequency and scaling sales velocity rapidly.

Thirdly, Seamless Integration of Cashless Settlement Networks: Business expansion relies heavily on frictionless transaction processing. By integrating secure mobile payment channels, 'Bikikini' minimizes the administrative risks and collection delays associated with handling physical cash, creating an efficient operational pipeline that accelerates working capital turnover.

Fourthly, In conclusion, digital retail platforms serve as the primary catalyst for modern economic growth: They seamlessly bridge the gap between wholesale supply chains and household demands. By leveraging electronic technology to streamline customer acquisition and delivery logistics, 'Bikikini' demonstrates how modern digital frameworks can achieve exponential operational scaling, proving that technology-driven business models are indispensable for contemporary commercial development.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Zakiul is a businessman. He has to talk on his mobile phone in various places for business purposes. When his mobile balance runs out, he has to travel a long distance to top it up (Flexiload). Therefore, his friend advised him to open an account with bKash or Rocket. Following the advice, he decided to open an account.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is the full form of LAN?
questions.answer: The full form of LAN is Local Area Network.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by e-banking? Explain.
questions.answer:
E-banking (Electronic Banking) refers to a modern banking system that allows customers to conduct financial transactions digitally over secure electronic networks, primarily the internet, without needing to visit a physical bank branch. It covers services like automated teller machines (ATMs), online fund transfers, mobile banking, and electronic bill payments.

In modern commerce, e-banking provides 24/7 financial access, improves transaction transparency, and greatly reduces processing times. It serves as the financial backbone for contemporary trade, enabling businesses and individuals to instantly settle invoices and process cashless transactions globally.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What kind of banking service do bKash or Rocket provide? Explain in the light of the stem.
questions.answer:
bKash and Rocket provide a modern digital financial service known as Mobile Banking (also termed Mobile Financial Services - MFS). This banking system uses telecommunication networks, cellular handsets, and digital application protocols to execute instant financial transfers and transactions remotely, bypassing traditional physical bank branches.

In the modern economy, mobile banking acts as a primary tool for financial inclusion and operational convenience. By converting a standard mobile phone into a personal digital wallet, it allows users to transfer funds, pay bills, cash in/out, and instantly purchase mobile airtime (talk-time/data) at any time from any location.

The stem highlights that Mr. Zakiul, a busy businessman, frequently runs out of mobile balance and has to travel long distances just to top up his phone (Flexiload). To solve this hassle, his friend recommends opening a bKash or Rocket account. Since these platforms allow him to perform instant mobile recharges and manage funds directly from his own handset via a cellular network, their services perfectly represent mobile banking.

questions.label: d
questions.text: How logical do you think Mr. Zakiul's decision to open an account is? Provide your opinion.
questions.answer:
Mr. Zakiul's decision to open a mobile banking account with bKash or Rocket is highly logical, timely, and essential for maximizing his business efficiency. In a fast-paced commercial environment, managing time and maintaining uninterrupted communication channels are critical factors for corporate success.

Firstly, Elimination of Distance and Time Bottlenecks: As a businessman, Mr. Zakiul has to travel and communicate constantly. Traveling a long distance just to do a manual mobile recharge wastes valuable business hours and incurs unnecessary transit costs. A mobile financial account will bring airtime top-up services directly to his handset, saving time and allowing him to focus on core business operations.

Secondly, Ensuring Uninterrupted Business Communication: In business, a disconnected phone line due to a low balance can lead to missed client calls, delayed deals, and financial losses. Having a bKash or Rocket account ensures that Mr. Zakiul can instantly recharge his mobile phone 24/7 from anywhere, maintaining continuous communication with his buyers, suppliers, and partners without any disruption.

Thirdly, Access to a Broader Digital Payment Ecosystem: Beyond simple mobile recharges, opening an MFS account provides Mr. Zakiul with a versatile business tool. He can use it to receive instant payments from customers, pay utility bills, transfer funds to suppliers, and manage micro-cash flows safely without carrying large amounts of physical cash, reducing the risk of theft or loss.

Fourthly, In conclusion, digital financial technology is indispensable for modern business management: It replaces slow, manual tasks with instantaneous digital workflows. Mr. Zakiul's decision to adopt mobile banking is completely justified as it optimizes his daily schedule, cuts operational hassles, and strengthens his competitive edge in the market.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Sabbir is the Chief Executive of 'ABC Bank'. He introduced a rule in his bank to record all transaction statements in computers using a specialized system instead of writing them in printed ledgers. Instead of managing customer account information through physical letters, he introduced an e-mail and mobile phone SMS alert system. Due to the improved customer service, the popularity of his bank has grown extensively.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is an e-mail?
questions.answer: E-mail (Electronic Mail) is a method of exchanging digital messages between people using digital devices such as computers and smartphones across internet networks or local communication channels.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by minimum subscription? Explain.
questions.answer: Minimum subscription (ন্যূনতম চাঁদা) refers to the minimum amount of capital defined in the prospectus that a public limited company must collect through public share applications before it can legally commence its business operations. According to corporate regulations, if a company fails to raise this designated amount within a specified time frame from launching its initial public offering (IPO), it cannot receive the certificate of commencement and must return all collected application money to the prospective investors.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which type of banking operations did Mr. Sabbir introduce? Explain.
questions.answer:
Mr. Sabbir has introduced Electronic Banking (E-Banking) operations, specifically focusing on Core Banking Solutions (CBS) and automated digital communication tracking. This system replaces traditional manual, paper-bound bookkeeping with central computer servers, data management software, and electronic communication channels to process financial data seamlessly.

In modern banking ecology, e-banking marks a transition away from traditional desktop files and physical paper ledgers. By converting customer records into secure digital profiles, financial institutions can instantly calculate interest, track account balances, and authorize payments without manual human entries, ensuring zero clerical errors and 24/7 service tracking.

The stem notes that Mr. Sabbir ordered all financial entries to be stored via a specialized computer network instead of printed books. Furthermore, he replaced physical mail communications with automated e-mail statements and real-time mobile SMS alerts. Because these services utilize an electronic infrastructure to store and transmit account details instantly, his banking method perfectly represents e-banking.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Will the method adopted in the stem be capable of providing improved service to customers? Write with arguments.
questions.answer:
Yes, I completely agree with the view that the technology-driven banking methods adopted by Mr. Sabbir are highly capable of providing superior, modern, and efficient customer services through several key dimensions:

Firstly, Real-Time Transaction Transparency and Account Security: Under older manual communication systems, clients had to wait days for paper letters to verify their balances, leaving accounts vulnerable to unauthorized changes or internal fraud. Real-time mobile SMS alerts and e-mail statements provide instant transaction alerts, giving consumers complete financial security over their assets.

Secondly, Drastic Reduction in Processing Delays and Waiting Time: Recording accounts in physical ledger books requires bank officers to manually search through shelves, resulting in long queues and wasting hours of valuable customer time. Storing transaction history on a centralized computer network allows bank tellers to retrieve data within seconds, accelerating service speed and reducing lobby congestion.

Thirdly, Frictionless and Cost-Effective Communication Networks: Traditional physical paperwork involves processing, printing, and postal transportation overheads, which indirectly raises operational service charges for customers. Shifting to digital communication alternatives completely removes print overheads and shipping hassles, enabling ABC Bank to provide swift support services at zero extra cost to the consumer.

Fourthly, In conclusion, electronic banking serves as the primary driver for modern consumer satisfaction and organizational expansion: It replaces slow, error-prone manual frameworks with streamlined digital systems. The rapid growth of ABC Bank's market popularity proves that Mr. Sabbir's modern operational upgrades directly fulfill the efficiency demands of contemporary retail banking consumers.

fullCQData.stem: Haydar's father sends him money every month. Haydar can go to a nearby bKash agent and withdraw the money using a mobile phone. Seeing Haydar receive this type of banking service, others decided to open bKash accounts as well.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is online banking?
questions.answer: Online banking is an electronic payment system that enables customers of a bank or other financial institution to conduct a range of financial transactions through the institution's secure website or online portal.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Explain the impact of information and communication technology on the modernization of business.
questions.answer:
Information and Communication Technology (ICT) plays a revolutionary role in modernizing businesses by enhancing operational speed, reducing structural overheads, and eliminating geographical boundaries. Through automation, digital record-keeping, and enterprise resource planning software, businesses can eliminate manual clerical errors and optimize supply chain pipelines.

Furthermore, the integration of digital platforms allows enterprises to execute global real-time communication, implement precision e-marketing strategies, and offer seamless online payment solutions. This shifts traditional commerce away from localized brick-and-mortar limitations and into a highly efficient, data-driven digital ecosystem.

questions.label: c
questions.text: In light of the stem, what type of banking service does 'bKash' provide? Explain.
questions.answer:
bKash provides a prominent digital financial service known as Mobile Banking (Mobile Financial Services - MFS). This modern banking system utilizes telecommunication networks, secure cellular applications, and mobile devices to execute financial transactions remotely without requiring users to visit physical bank branches.

In modern financial frameworks, mobile banking bridges the gap between formal banking channels and the unbanked population. By converting a standard cellular number into a personal digital wallet, it enables instant money transfers, real-time bill payments, and cashless merchant transactions through authorized local agent points.

The stem highlights that Haydar receives money sent by his father every month and withdraws it by visiting a nearby bKash agent using a mobile phone. Because this entire transaction cycle of sending and cashing out funds is securely executed over a cellular network infrastructure using mobile technology, the service perfectly matches the definition of mobile banking.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the logical justification of Haydar's father sending money through bKash.
questions.answer:
Sending money through bKash is highly logical, time-saving, and fully justified from a socioeconomic perspective, as it solves the traditional hurdles of long-distance fund transfers through several key dimensions:

Firstly, Instant Transmission of Funds: Traditional remittance methods, such as postal money orders or conventional bank deposits, often take days to process and clear. By using bKash, Haydar's father can transfer funds in real time, ensuring that Haydar receives his living or educational expenses instantly, which is vital during emergencies.

Secondly, Unmatched Convenience and Accessibility: Conventional banking infrastructure requires both the sender and the receiver to visit physical town branches within restrictive operating hours, leading to long queues and wasted time. Shifting to an MFS network allows Haydar's father to send money from anywhere, while Haydar can easily withdraw it from a nearby neighborhood agent without incurring travel expenses.

Thirdly, Enhanced Financial Security and Reliability: Carrying physical cash over long distances presents significant security vulnerabilities, such as theft, hijacking, or extortion. The digital financial framework protects transactions with personalized PIN numbers and automated ledger tracking, ensuring that the hard-earned money goes directly into Haydar's account safely and transparently.

Fourthly, In conclusion, mobile financial technology serves as an indispensable tool for enhancing consumer welfare: It simplifies family support networks and cuts transaction delays. The fact that Haydar's neighbors decided to open bKash accounts after witnessing his hassle-free experience proves that this method is the most efficient, logical, and practical solution for managing recurring domestic payments.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Bulbul is a union-level electronic goods businessman. He sells TVs, refrigerators, mobile phones, washing machines, etc. For some time, he noticed that the sales of his showroom have been decreasing day by day. He is very frustrated about this matter. Without collecting and verifying information regarding the modern technological knowledge of the consumers in that region, he advertised the products sold by his enterprise on the internet and Facebook. However, even after that, the sales of his enterprise did not increase.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is an e-mail?
questions.answer: E-mail (Electronic Mail) is a method of exchanging digital messages between individuals using electronic devices such as computers and smartphones over internet networks or communication channels.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by re-export? Explain.
questions.answer:
Re-export (পুনঃরপ্তানি) refers to the commercial practice of importing raw materials or finished commodities from a foreign nation and then exporting them to a third country, either in their original form or after carrying out further processing, packaging, or value addition.

In international trade, re-exporting allows landlocked nations or countries lacking direct trade agreements to access global distribution pipelines. It helps intermediate trading hubs generate foreign currency revenue by leveraging their superior logistics infrastructure, maritime ports, and specialized manufacturing processing zones without relying on domestic natural resource cultivation.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which medium of communication did Mr. Bulbul use in his business as described in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
Mr. Bulbul used a modern electronic communication framework known as Digital Media and Internet-based Communication Channels, specifically utilizing E-marketing (Electronic Marketing) via Social Media Networks (Facebook) and web platforms. This medium uses internet-enabled infrastructure to transmit commercial advertisements and product data to a target audience remotely.

In modern business dynamics, digital communication channels act as virtual promotional boards. Instead of depending on traditional paper leaflets, regional newspapers, or physical signboards, enterprises use online platforms to display product specifications, pricing, and visual advertisements directly to users connected to the network.

The stem highlights that in an attempt to boost his dropping showroom sales, Mr. Bulbul launched promotional campaigns by publishing advertisements for his electronic items (TVs, refrigerators, mobile phones) on the internet and Facebook. Since these marketing messages were published and distributed electronically across digital networks, his chosen communication medium represents e-marketing.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Analyze the core reason behind the lack of change in Mr. Bulbul's business situation.
questions.answer:
The core reason behind the lack of improvement in Mr. Bulbul's business sales is his failure to conduct a market survey and his poor choice of marketing channels relative to his consumers' digital literacy (ক্রেতাদের আধুনিক প্রযুক্তির জ্ঞান ও পরিবেশ বিশ্লেষণ না করা). Effective marketing requires aligning the communication medium with the actual habits and technological competencies of the local consumer segment.

Firstly, Complete Neglect of Environmental Analysis and Consumer Readiness: In managerial economics, launching an advertising campaign without evaluating market literacy inevitably results in capital waste. Mr. Bulbul operates at the union level, where rural or grassroots consumers may have limited access to high-speed internet, low digital literacy, or minimal familiarity with discovering consumer appliances through social media feeds.

Secondly, Mismatched Communication Channel Selection: Electronic appliances like refrigerators and washing machines are high-involvement purchases for rural households. If local buyers are accustomed to traditional face-to-face interactions, physical product inspections, and local word-of-mouth recommendations, broad digital ads on Facebook will fail to influence their purchasing decisions.

Thirdly, Ignoring the Core Quality of Local Target Marketing: Mr. Bulbul failed to analyze why his showroom sales were declining in the first place—which could be due to issues like poor after-sales service, uncompetitive local pricing, or aggressive competitor behavior. Instead of addressing the underlying operational issues, he deployed a generic digital solution that missed his target demographic entirely.

Fourthly, In conclusion, digital tools are only effective when tailored to the demographic realities of the consumer base: Implementing modern business technologies requires a thorough assessment of local market conditions. Mr. Bulbul’s failure to cross-check the technological readiness of his union-level consumers before investing in online ads is the primary reason his business situation stagnated.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Azad opened an account at the Dhaka branch of Sangu Bank. He uses paper documents such as receipts and checks when transacting with this account. There is always a sufficient balance deposited in his account. Mr. Majid opened an account at the Rangpur branch of Kushiyara Bank. He does not use any paper documents for bank transactions. Both of them went on a family vacation to Cox's Bazar, where both banks have branches. Both went to their respective bank branches to withdraw money. Majid successfully withdrew his money properly. Although Azad presented his check following all standard rules, the bank failed to provide him with the necessary service because it was a check from a different branch.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is B2C?
questions.answer: B2C (Business-to-Consumer) is an electronic business model that involves commercial transactions conducted directly between a business enterprise and individual end consumers over electronic networks like the internet.

questions.label: b
questions.text: What is meant by a partner by estoppel? Explain.
questions.answer:
A partner by estoppel (প্রতিবন্ধ অংশীদার) refers to an individual who is not legally a partner in a firm, nor do they hold any capital or share in its profits, but who intentionally represents themselves as a partner through their words, behavior, or written declarations.

According to partnership laws, if third parties extend loans or credit to the firm based on such a representation, the partner by estoppel becomes personally and legally liable for those debts. They cannot later deny their partnership status to escape financial liability, as their own actions led outsiders to trust the firm's creditworthiness.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What kind of banking service is Kushiyara Bank providing in the stem? Explain from a technological perspective.
questions.answer:
Kushiyara Bank is providing a modern technological framework known as Online Banking (specifically utilizing Core Banking Solutions - CBS). This system connects all branches of a financial institution to a centralized master network database, allowing real-time data access and transaction processing across any branch nationwide.

From a technological perspective, traditional banking stores customer accounts in localized, isolated branch databases. Online banking completely eliminates these geographic barriers by synchronizing data on a central cloud or mainframe server. This allows transactions to be fully paperless, secure, and independent of the specific branch where the account was originally registered.

The stem highlights that Mr. Majid opened his account at the Rangpur branch of Kushiyara Bank but conducts transactions completely paperless. When he went to Cox's Bazar, he safely withdrew his money from that local branch without any interruption. Because the bank successfully processed his transaction away from his home branch through a digital network, it demonstrates a classic application of online core banking.

questions.label: d
questions.text: What are the necessary steps to eliminate the failure of Sangu Bank in providing services? Give your opinion with arguments.
questions.answer:
To eliminate its service failures and meet modern consumer demands, Sangu Bank must immediately transition from traditional manual banking to an integrated Online Banking System using Core Banking Solutions (CBS).

Firstly, Eliminating Branch-Centric Geographic Barriers: Sangu Bank currently operates on an outdated decentralized framework where an account is strictly bound to its issuing branch. By implementing CBS, all branches will be linked to a single central database server, transforming the institution from a collection of isolated branches into an interconnected national banking network.

Secondly, Enabling Real-Time Anywhere Banking for Consumers: As seen in the stem, Mr. Azad faced a service failure during his vacation because Sangu Bank could not verify his balance or process an out-of-station physical check. If Sangu Bank adopts online banking, a customer can walk into any branch across the country—such as the Cox's Bazar branch—and withdraw cash, deposit funds, or verify transactions instantly.

Thirdly, Reducing Reliance on Vulnerable Paper Transactions: Depending entirely on paper checks and receipts increases processing delays, clerical verification errors, and administrative overheads. Upgrading to electronic channels (like ATMs, debit cards, and internet portals) will allow Sangu Bank to process transactions instantly and securely, ensuring customers have continuous access to their funds.

Fourthly, In conclusion, digital modernization is essential for market survival and consumer retention: Traditional manual banking is highly inefficient and leads to customer dissatisfaction. Upgrading to a comprehensive online network is the only logical path for Sangu Bank to eliminate operational failures, match competitor standards like Kushiyara Bank, and secure long-term consumer trust.

fullCQData.stem: Mrs. Sahana is a banker. She has to work in her office until 5 PM. Moreover, she is afraid of three-way traffic jams, long queues, and moving from one shop to another. Therefore, on this Eid, she completed her clothing shopping from the comfort of her home via the internet.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is e-banking?
questions.answer: E-banking (Electronic Banking) refers to a modern banking service that enables customers to conduct financial transactions digitally over secure electronic networks, primarily the internet, without visiting a physical brick-and-mortar bank branch.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Why is mobile banking important? Explain.
questions.answer:
Mobile banking is highly important because it drives financial inclusion and provides unparalleled transaction convenience, speed, and safety. It eliminates geographic barriers and restrictive banking hours, allowing users to transfer funds, pay utility bills, and check balances instantly 24/7 from any location using a cellular handset.

Additionally, it minimizes transactional overheads for banks and reduces travel costs and physical security risks for consumers, making it an essential tool in modern digital economics.

questions.label: c
questions.text: Which field of online business is described in the stem? Explain.
questions.answer:
The online business domain described in the stem is E-commerce (Electronic Commerce), specifically falling under the Business-to-Consumer (B2C) trade model. This refers to the organizational framework where commercial enterprises utilize web pages, social networks, and internet applications to market, sell, and distribute products directly to individual end consumers.

In modern business economics, e-commerce redefines the consumer purchasing lifecycle. By replacing physical retail storefronts with virtual product catalogs, businesses can present interactive imagery, sizing specifications, and transparent pricing structures directly to remote buyers, eliminating middle tier distributors and retail space rental costs.

The stem outlines that Mrs. Sahana, due to her rigid office hours and fear of traffic congestion, opted out of physical market shopping. Instead, she used an internet connection from her home to browse and complete her Eid clothing purchases. Since this transaction involves an individual buyer selecting and procuring consumer items directly from an online retail entity over an electronic network, it represents a textbook application of B2C e-commerce.

questions.label: d
questions.text: Explain the necessity of using ICT in business in the light of the stem.
questions.answer:
The stem strongly highlights that the integration of Information and Communication Technology (ICT) is absolutely necessary for modern business operations because it optimizes consumer time management, lowers transaction friction, and unlocks structural scaling capabilities through several key dimensions:

Firstly, Overcoming Consumer Time Constraints and Accessibility Barriers: Traditional brick-and-mortar commerce requires customers to visit retail outlets during set business hours. For working professionals like Mrs. Sahana who work until 5 PM, physical shopping becomes a heavy operational burden. ICT solves this problem by providing 24/7 online storefronts, allowing businesses to capture revenue from busy demographics at any time of day.

Secondly, Eliminating Structural Hassles and Transit Friction: Urban physical markets face challenges like major traffic jams, long checkout lines, and the exhaustion of moving between different physical shops. By deploying e-commerce platforms, businesses bring the marketplace straight to the consumer's digital screen, turning external urban challenges into an efficient, home-based shopping experience.

Thirdly, Drastic Reduction in Corporate Operating Costs: Conventional retail expansion demands massive capital investments in prime storefront rentals, showroom interior designs, and local sales staff. By leveraging ICT tools like web portals, databases, and digital payment networks, an enterprise can manage a national inventory from a central warehouse, turning high fixed overheads into higher profit margins.

Fourthly, In conclusion, ICT is an indispensable tool for enhancing market efficiency and consumer satisfaction: It seamlessly connects commercial supply chains with changing lifestyle demands. Mrs. Sahana’s shift to online shopping proves that businesses must adopt digital transaction infrastructures to stay competitive, increase customer retention, and achieve sustainable growth in today's economy.

fullCQData.stem: Mr. Anik Ahmed opened a deposit account at the Gulshan branch of Swadeshi Bank. He uses paper-based documents such as checks, receipts, etc., while managing his account. There is always a sufficient balance deposited in his account. On the other hand, Mr. Jamil opened an account at the Khulna branch of Rupsha Bank. He does not use any paper-based documents for bank transactions. One day, both of them went on a family vacation to Kuakata, where both banks have branches. They tried to withdraw money from their respective bank branches there. Mr. Jamil successfully withdrew his money properly. However, although Anik Ahmed presented his check following all standard rules, the bank failed to provide him with instant service because it was a check from a different branch.

questions.label: a
questions.text: What is creativity?
questions.answer: Creativity (সৃজনশীলতা) is the ability to generate new, unique, and valuable ideas, concepts, solutions, or products by connecting existing ideas in innovative ways.

questions.label: b
questions.text: Self-employment is not possible without business entrepreneurship—Explain.
questions.answer:
Self-employment and business entrepreneurship are deeply interconnected, as one cannot exist without the other. Self-employment means creating one's own employment opportunity instead of relying on a traditional job. However, simply coming up with a self-employment idea is not enough; turning that idea into reality requires business entrepreneurship.

Entrepreneurship involves taking the initiative, gathering necessary resources, bearing financial and operational risks, and setting up an enterprise to deliver a product or service. Without this entrepreneurial drive, risk-taking ability, and organization, a self-employment concept remains a mere thought and can never transform into an active, functional livelihood.

questions.label: c
questions.text: What kind of banking service is Rupsha Bank providing as described in the stem? Describe.
questions.answer:
Rupsha Bank is providing a modern technological financial framework known as Online Banking (specifically utilizing Core Banking Solutions - CBS). This integrated system connects all geographic branches of a financial institution to a single centralized master network database, enabling real-time data access, synchronization, and secure transaction processing across any branch nationwide.

From a technological standpoint, traditional banking keeps customer financial data stored in isolated, localized branch servers, which creates massive spatial limitations. Online core banking completely eliminates these geographic barriers by managing records on a central mainframe or cloud server. This allows transactions to be fully paperless, swift, and entirely independent of the specific branch where the account was originally registered.

The stem highlights that Mr. Jamil opened his account at the Khulna branch of Rupsha Bank but conducts completely paperless transactions. When he went to Kuakata on a vacation, he successfully withdrew his funds from the local Kuakata branch without any disruption or delay. Because Rupsha Bank securely processed his withdrawal away from his home branch via a centralized digital communication network, its infrastructure represents a classic application of online core banking.

questions.label: d
questions.text: What steps should be taken to eliminate the reasons behind Swadeshi Bank's service failure in the stem? Give your opinion with arguments.
questions.answer:
To eliminate its service failures and adapt to modern consumer demands, Swadeshi Bank must immediately transition from traditional branch-bound banking to an integrated Online Banking System using Core Banking Solutions (CBS).

Firstly, Eliminating Branch-Centric Geographic Barriers: Swadeshi Bank currently operates on an outdated decentralized framework where an account's financial record is strictly bound to its issuing branch. By implementing CBS, all regional branches will link to a single central database server, transforming the bank from a collection of isolated offices into an interconnected nationwide banking network.

Secondly, Enabling Real-Time "Anywhere Banking" for Consumers: As shown in the stem, Mr. Anik faced a severe service failure during his vacation because Swadeshi Bank's Kuakata branch could not instantly verify his Gulshan branch balance or process an out-of-station physical check. If Swadeshi Bank adopts online banking, a customer can walk into any branch across the country—such as the Kuakata branch—and withdraw cash or verify balances instantly.

Thirdly, Reducing Reliance on Paper Transactions: Depending entirely on physical checks and manual receipts increases processing delays, verification errors, and administrative friction. Upgrading to automated electronic channels (like ATMs, debit cards, and internet portals) will allow Swadeshi Bank to process transactions instantly and securely, ensuring customers have 24/7 access to their funds.

Fourthly, In conclusion, digital modernization is essential for market survival and consumer retention: Traditional manual banking is highly inefficient and leads to customer dissatisfaction. Upgrading to a comprehensive online network is the only logical path for Swadeshi Bank to eliminate operational failures, match competitor standards like Rupsha Bank, and secure long-term consumer trust.
